- Bitcoin has declined over 5.5% in five days, dropping from highs above $77,000 to roughly $72,600.
- BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) recorded $527.84 million in net outflows on Wednesday, its second-largest single-day withdrawal.
- Total U.S. spot Bitcoin ETF outflows reached $733.43 million on Wednesday, the highest daily combined total since late January.
- A single investor executed a $1.29 billion dark-pool block trade of IBIT shares on Tuesday, contributing to record-high ETF trading volumes.
- Analysts attribute the volatility to a combination of geopolitical instability and a mechanical "cooling" period for institutional investors.

### Why it Matters
The shift toward institutional ETF ownership has transformed Bitcoin into a market governed by mechanical, high-volume trading rather than purely speculative narratives. This infrastructure now acts as a shock absorber, but it also facilitates rapid capital outflows that can amplify price volatility during periods of global uncertainty.

author: Louise Byrne
title: Canaries in the coal mine? How AI could reshape work in Ireland
summary:
Rising artificial intelligence adoption is reshaping Ireland’s labor market, causing significant uncertainty for graduates and entry-level workers as companies shift hiring strategies and automate traditional professional roles.

### Key Points
- IrishJobs reports that nearly 50% of Irish employers have reduced entry and graduate-level roles this year.
- Ireland has lost approximately 20,000 ICT jobs in the past 12 months, with companies citing both post-pandemic corrections and AI integration.
- The Department of Finance and the IMF identify Ireland as one of the economies most exposed to AI-driven labor disruption.
- Recruitment firm Morgan McKinley notes the emergence of over 80 new AI-specific job titles, such as AI Ethics and Data Curation, to offset losses.
- Ibec is calling for the utilization of the €2 billion National Training Fund surplus to support urgent worker reskilling and education initiatives.

### Why it Matters
The rapid integration of AI into the workforce creates a skills gap that threatens to leave new graduates and entry-level employees without viable career pathways. While some experts argue that new roles will emerge to replace those lost, the speed of this transition requires immediate government and educational intervention to ensure the labor market remains competitive and resilient.

Ubiquiti has released critical security patches for six vulnerabilities, while other major industry developments include a botnet takedown, new phishing threats, and emerging hardware-based tracking techniques.

### Key Points
- Ubiquiti patched six security flaws in its equipment, including one critical vulnerability rated 9.1 and another scoring a perfect 10.0 on the CVE scale.
- A coalition including CrowdStrike and Google successfully disrupted the Glassworm botnet, which targeted open-source repositories like NPM and PyPI to steal credentials.
- The FBI issued a warning regarding Kali365, a "Phishing-as-a-Service" platform that automates credential theft for Microsoft 365 accounts.
- Researchers identified a new side-channel attack called FROST, which uses SSD performance timing via JavaScript to fingerprint devices and monitor user activity.
- Microsoft is enhancing C# memory safety in .NET 11 to prevent common vulnerabilities like use-after-free errors by adopting stricter object lifetime enforcement.

### Why it Matters
These developments highlight the persistent risks within both enterprise network infrastructure and the global software supply chain. As attackers increasingly leverage automated services and sophisticated side-channel techniques, organizations must prioritize rapid patch management and adopt modern memory-safe programming practices to maintain security.
title: This Week in Security: Ubiquiti Fixes, and FreeBSD Joins the Club you Don’t Want to Join
author: Mike Kershaw

summary:
Cybersecurity researchers discovered a malicious NuGet package named Sicoob.Sdk that impersonates a legitimate Brazilian banking tool to steal sensitive PFX certificates and client IDs from unsuspecting software developers.

### Key Points
- The malicious package, versions 2.0.0 through 2.0.4, was downloaded nearly 500 times before being blocked by NuGet.
- Attackers used the package to exfiltrate PFX certificates, passwords, and Boleto API transaction data to a hardcoded Sentry endpoint.
- Google Search AI Mode inadvertently amplified the threat by surfacing the malicious package as a legitimate C# library for Sicoob banking APIs.
- The threat actor behind the "sicoob" profile published 11 other packages, totaling approximately 6,000 downloads across the ecosystem.
- Affected organizations are advised to rotate all PFX credentials and audit API logs for unauthorized access or unusual activity.

### Why it Matters
This incident highlights the growing danger of "manufactured legitimacy," where attackers create convincing packages that bypass traditional security filters and developer scrutiny. Such supply chain compromises can lead to widespread financial fraud and the unauthorized exposure of sensitive corporate banking data.
title: Malicious Sicoob NuGet Steals Banking Credentials as npm Packages Target Cloud Secrets
author: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

summary:
The rapid expansion of AI-powered data centers has triggered a bipartisan grassroots backlash across the United States, pitting local community concerns against the massive infrastructure needs of Big Tech.

### Key Points
- A May Gallup poll indicates that 70 percent of Americans oppose the construction of AI data centers within their local communities.
- Tech giants including Microsoft, Amazon, Meta, and Google have invested more in data centers since the launch of ChatGPT than the federal government spent on the interstate-highway system.
- Local opposition led to the cancellation of at least 20 proposed data center projects during the first quarter of 2026.
- Residents frequently cite noise pollution, excessive water consumption, and the secrecy of real-estate shell companies as primary drivers for their opposition.
- Political figures ranging from Bernie Sanders and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez to populist conservatives have expressed skepticism or support for moratoriums on new facility construction.

### Why it Matters
Data centers have become a potent symbol of the perceived disconnect between wealthy tech elites and the daily realities of average citizens. This emerging conflict threatens to disrupt the AI industry's growth trajectory and will likely become a significant, polarizing issue in upcoming midterm and 2028 presidential elections.
title: Why Everyone Hates AI Data Centers
author: Charlie Warzel

summary:
AI training startup Shift is offering free home cleaning services in New York and London in exchange for video footage used to teach robots how to perform domestic chores.

### Key Points
- Shift collects "egocentric" or first-person video data of cleaners performing tasks like scrubbing, dusting, and mopping to train physical AI models.
- Robotics companies face a data bottleneck because physical tasks involving friction, force, and spatial awareness are difficult to scrape from the internet.
- The startup Human Archive is scaling data collection by having gig workers record their activities while wearing specialized camera-equipped caps.
- Home services platform Pronto has faced backlash for recording footage of clients performing chores like cooking and laundry to develop AI training sets.
- Some companies operate "data farms" where workers are paid to repeat simple physical tasks, such as folding towels, while sensors capture their movements.

### Why it Matters
This trend highlights a shift toward commodifying private domestic behavior to overcome the significant technical challenges of training robots for the physical world. As companies race to automate household labor, the practice raises critical questions regarding consumer privacy and the ethics of using personal living spaces as data collection sites.
title: Tech companies desperately want to film you doing chores
author: Robert Hart

summary:
Micron, Samsung, and SK Hynix have reached a combined $1 trillion market valuation as surging demand for artificial intelligence hardware transforms the historically volatile memory-chip industry.

### Key Points
- Micron, Samsung, and SK Hynix now effectively control the global DRAM market, reducing the industry's historic tendency toward overproduction.
- AI systems require massive amounts of data processing, creating persistent memory bottlenecks that drive demand for high-performance chips.
- UBS analysts project the DRAM market will remain undersupplied through 2028 due to long-term supply agreements with major cloud computing providers.
- Samsung reported over $30 billion in profit during the first quarter of this year, signaling strong financial performance across the sector.
- Industry consolidation has reduced the number of major DRAM manufacturers from over 20 in the 1990s to just three primary players today.

### Why it Matters
The shift toward a consolidated market and long-term pricing contracts suggests the memory industry may be moving away from its traditional cycle of boom-and-bust volatility. This stability could fundamentally change how investors value semiconductor companies as they become essential infrastructure for the global AI economy.
title: Short memory? The most dangerous words in investing are back
author: Alistair Barr

summary:
Apple will integrate a new variable aperture lens into the iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max, increasing camera component costs by 50% compared to current iPhone 17 Pro models.

### Key Points
- The variable aperture lens allows physical adjustments to light intake, improving exposure control and depth of field flexibility.
- Supply chain analyst Ming-Chi Kuo reports that Sunny Optical will supply 40% to 50% of these new camera module orders.
- Apple doubled its 2026 shipment forecast for the MacBook Neo to 10 million units following stronger-than-expected market performance.
- Future 2028 iPhone models are expected to adopt chip-on-board packaging for ultra-wide cameras to improve space efficiency and image quality.
- Sunny Optical has secured component contracts for two upcoming OpenAI hardware products, including a new smartphone and a mobile device.

### Why it Matters
The shift to variable aperture technology represents a significant hardware investment that could differentiate future iPhone Pro models in a competitive smartphone market. This supply chain expansion also highlights Sunny Optical's growing influence as a key manufacturing partner for both Apple’s hardware ecosystem and emerging AI-focused devices.
title: iPhone 18 Pro's Camera Upgrade Will Cost Apple 50% More
author: Hartley Charlton

summary:
California Attorney General Rob Bonta has filed a lawsuit against 23andMe, alleging the company failed to protect the sensitive genetic and personal data of nearly seven million users.

### Key Points
- The lawsuit targets Chrome Holding Co., the corporate entity representing 23andMe following its recent bankruptcy proceedings.
- Hackers allegedly accessed user accounts for five months in 2023, including ancestry and genetic health information sold on the dark web.
- Court documents claim the company ignored over one million unauthorized login attempts to a single account in one day.
- 23andMe previously agreed to a $30 million settlement to resolve a separate class-action lawsuit regarding the same data breach.
- The company was recently sold for $305 million to the TTAM Research Institute, a nonprofit led by former CEO Anne Wojcicki.

### Why it Matters
This legal action highlights the significant privacy risks associated with storing highly sensitive biological data in the consumer genetic testing industry. The case underscores the potential for severe regulatory consequences when companies fail to implement adequate cybersecurity safeguards for their customers' most personal information.
title: 23andMe accused of failing to protect user data in new lawsuit
author: Katie Kindelan, Vera Drymon

summary:
**Author Steve Rosenbaum faces scrutiny after his book about AI-driven misinformation, "The Future of Truth," was found to contain synthetic quotes and significant AI-generated content.**

### Key Points
- Steve Rosenbaum admitted to using AI tools like ChatGPT and Claude for research, brainstorming, and language refinement in his book.
- *The New York Times* identified over six made-up or misattributed quotes within the text of the book.
- AI-detection software Pangram estimated the book was 53 percent AI-generated, with an additional 9 percent likely AI-assisted.
- WIRED retracted its excerpt of the book after questions arose regarding the extent of Rosenbaum's reliance on generative AI tools.
- Rosenbaum maintains that his core ideas and reporting are original, despite his refusal to clarify which specific passages were AI-assisted.

### Why it Matters
This controversy highlights the growing tension between traditional editorial standards and the increasing integration of generative AI in the publishing industry. It underscores the difficulty of verifying authorship and accuracy in an era where AI tools can easily blur the lines between human-led research and synthetic content.
title: We Asked the ‘Future of Truth’ Author to Explain How He Used AI. It Didn’t Go Well
author: Kate Knibbs

summary:
Tony Blair’s recent essay advocating for radical economic deregulation and AI integration has sparked debate over his influence on the Keir Starmer government and ties to Oracle.

### Key Points
- Tony Blair’s essay calls for pension cuts, NHS privatization, and the abandonment of Net Zero climate commitments to prioritize cheap energy for AI data centers.
- Oracle founder Larry Ellison has invested over $348 million into the Tony Blair Institute for Global Change since 2021, raising concerns regarding corporate lobbying.
- The Starmer government has implemented "AI Growth Zones" that bypass standard environmental assessments and community planning regulations to accelerate data center construction.
- Blair’s policy recommendations include strengthening ties with the Trump administration and expanding the UK’s role in US-led military operations in the Middle East.
- Public opposition is mounting against government proposals for mandatory digital identity systems, which critics argue are designed for mass surveillance and control.

### Why it Matters
The alignment between Blair’s policy agenda and the commercial interests of his primary benefactor, Larry Ellison, highlights growing concerns over corporate influence on British public policy. This relationship suggests a shift toward a surveillance-heavy, deregulated economy that prioritizes AI infrastructure over traditional environmental and social safeguards.
title: In His “Radical Centralism” Essay, Tony Blair Is, As Always, Talking His Book
author: Nick Corbishley

summary:
Fenris Creations has partnered with Google DeepMind to integrate AI research into EVE Online, aiming to modernize the space MMO’s aging codebase and enhance non-player character interactions.

### Key Points
- Fenris Creations, formerly CCP Games, granted Google DeepMind a minority stake to train AI agents on an offline version of EVE Online.
- Developers are utilizing AI "coaching agents" to identify memory and security issues within the game's 30-year-old "spaghetti code."
- The partnership aims to revitalize mission-giving NPCs by using generative AI to make character interactions feel more dynamic and alive.
- Despite widespread industry backlash against AI in gaming, EVE Online’s playerbase has remained largely receptive or cautiously curious toward the initiative.
- Google DeepMind previously gained recognition for developing Grandmaster-ranked AI agents for StarCraft 2 and mastering complex board games like Go.

### Why it Matters
This collaboration represents a rare instance where a dedicated gaming community has embraced AI integration, largely due to the potential for fixing long-standing technical debt. If successful, the project could provide a blueprint for using machine learning to maintain and evolve massive, legacy software environments without alienating core users.
title: "They have the biggest bullsh*t detectors on the planet": How the unlikely EVE Online x Google DeepMind AI partnership landed with players
author: James Archer

summary:
Intercontinental Exchange CEO Jeffrey Sprecher praised the decentralized platform Hyperliquid for its significant trading volume, highlighting the growing influence of crypto-native derivatives markets over traditional financial exchange incumbents.

### Key Points
- ICE CEO Jeffrey Sprecher stated that Hyperliquid’s trading activity in perpetual futures now exceeds that of the Nasdaq exchange.
- Hyperliquid currently controls over 70 percent of the decentralized perpetual futures market, clearing billions in daily notional turnover.
- The platform attracts traditional traders by offering 24/7 access to oil derivatives, including during weekends when regulated markets are closed.
- Sprecher noted that Hyperliquid operates as an unregulated offshore entity, creating a competitive disparity with firms bound by Dodd-Frank and EMIR regulations.
- ICE leadership has held multiple meetings with Hyperliquid’s core team, signaling a shift in how traditional Wall Street firms view crypto-native platforms.

### Why it Matters
The comparison highlights a growing regulatory and competitive tension between traditional exchanges and decentralized platforms that operate outside established financial frameworks. Policymakers now face pressure to either create new regulatory categories for perpetual futures or force offshore venues to comply with existing global derivatives laws.
title: Hyperliquid bigger than NASDAQ, says ICE CEO Jeffrey Sprecher
author: Shaurya Malwa

summary:
MSI has unveiled the Claw 8 AI Plus gaming handheld featuring Intel’s new Arc G3 Extreme processor, while industry news highlights major developments in gaming hardware and labor relations.

### Key Points
- The MSI Claw 8 AI Plus features an eight-inch 1920 x 1200 touchscreen, 32GB of LPDDR5x memory, and a redesigned ergonomic chassis.
- IO Interactive reported that its new title, 007 First Light, sold 1.5 million copies within the first 24 hours of release.
- Developers of Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 2 confirmed a new, highly requested character class will arrive in the first half of 2027.
- Rockstar Games staff formed the Rockstar Game Workers Union to protest the termination of 31 employees, which workers allege was union-busting.
- CD Projekt Red joint CEO Michał Nowakowski stated that The Witcher 4 will likely not receive massive expansions due to an ambitious three-game release schedule.

### Why it Matters
The entry of Intel’s Arc G3 Extreme processor into the handheld market signals a significant shift in competition against AMD, potentially influencing future device performance and pricing. Meanwhile, the formation of the Rockstar Game Workers Union reflects growing labor activism within the gaming industry that could impact future studio operations and development cycles.
title: Behold The Latest Very Cool, Super Deluxe PC Gaming Handheld You Probably Can’t Afford
author: Ethan Gach

summary:
The SwiftScan VIP mobile application is currently available for a discounted lifetime price of $47.97, offering users a comprehensive tool to digitize, edit, and organize physical documents on smartphones.

### Key Points
- SwiftScan VIP converts physical documents, receipts, and business cards into high-quality PDF or JPG files using mobile device cameras.
- The app features automatic edge detection, image enhancement, and multi-page scanning to streamline the digitization process.
- Integrated OCR technology makes scanned text searchable, while built-in tools allow users to sign, annotate, redact, and rearrange PDF pages.
- Scanned files can be uploaded directly to cloud storage services including iCloud, Google Drive, Dropbox, and Evernote.
- A lifetime subscription to the service is currently priced at $47.97, a significant reduction from the original $199.99 retail cost.

### Why it Matters
This offer provides a cost-effective solution for individuals and professionals looking to reduce physical paper clutter through mobile-first document management. By enabling seamless cloud integration and searchable digital archives, the tool improves personal productivity and simplifies long-term record keeping.
title: This app digitizes your paper clutter for $48 for life
author: Boing Boing's Shop

summary:
Google Chrome users on various Android tablets are reporting a persistent "You can have up to 5 windows" error message that prevents the browser from opening correctly.

### Key Points
- Affected devices include the Samsung Galaxy Tab S9 Plus, S9 FE, Galaxy Tab A11 Plus, Lenovo Tab M11, and Xiaomi Pad 6.
- The error message prevents the browser from launching, rendering the application unusable for many tablet owners.
- Google has officially acknowledged the bug on its IssueTracker and is currently gathering device data from impacted users.
- A temporary workaround involves uninstalling Chrome updates via the Google Play Store, though this action deletes all saved browser data.

### Why it Matters
This bug significantly disrupts the browsing experience for Android tablet users who rely on Chrome as their primary web interface. As Google investigates the root cause, the lack of a permanent fix forces users to either lose their saved data through a factory reset or switch to alternative browsers.
title: Android tablets have a problem: Chrome's not working, but Google's racing to fix it
author: nickodiaz@sbcglobal.net (Nickolas Diaz) , Nickolas Diaz

summary:
Kog has launched a high-speed inference engine that optimizes GPU software stacks to achieve 3,000 tokens per second for single-request AI agent workloads on standard datacenter hardware.

### Key Points
- The Kog Inference Engine achieves high speeds by replacing standard multi-kernel stacks with a "monokernel" that runs as a single, persistent GPU program.
- Performance is bottlenecked by memory bandwidth rather than compute power, allowing 8-GPU nodes to reach speeds previously reserved for dedicated inference hardware.
- The company’s "Laneformer" architecture uses Delayed Tensor Parallelism to overlap cross-device communication with computation, reducing latency in multi-GPU setups.
- A public tech preview is available at playground.kog.ai, demonstrating the engine's capabilities using a 2B-parameter coding model on an 8x AMD MI300X node.
- Kog is a Paris-based startup founded in 2023 that has raised $5 million to focus on low-level GPU engineering for autonomous AI agents.

### Why it Matters
As AI agents shift from simple chatbots to complex, iterative systems, the speed of single-request token generation has become the primary bottleneck for productivity. By unlocking the full memory bandwidth of existing datacenter GPUs, this technology enables faster reasoning and tool-use loops without requiring proprietary inference hardware.
title: Real-time LLM Inference on Standard GPUs: 3k tokens/s per request
author: Kog Team

summary:
Google Workspace has launched a beta program allowing administrators to convert client-side encrypted Google Slides files following a data export through Google Vault or Google Takeout services.

### Key Points
- The beta program enables interoperability for client-side encrypted (CSE) Slides after exporting data.
- Eligible editions include Enterprise Plus, Education Standard, Education Plus, Frontline Plus, and Assured Controls.
- Interested administrators must apply via a registration form to gain access to the CSE Office Interop beta.
- This update follows the recent general availability of similar conversion capabilities for Google Sheets files.
- The rollout is currently active for both Rapid Release and Scheduled Release domains upon successful beta acceptance.

### Why it Matters
This update simplifies data management for organizations that rely on client-side encryption for sensitive document security. By enabling file conversion after exports, Google provides administrators with greater flexibility when migrating or auditing encrypted content across enterprise environments.
title: Available in beta: Convert your client-side encrypted Slides after a Vault or Takeout export
author: Paul Jiménez Vela

summary:
Artificial intelligence is lowering technical barriers to entrepreneurship, offering a critical opportunity to close the gender gap in technology by enabling women to become creators rather than just users.

### Key Points
- No-code AI platforms allow individuals to build digital products and automate workflows without requiring traditional software engineering expertise or large development teams.
- AI literacy is becoming a fundamental skill for career progression and economic mobility across sectors including healthcare, education, and retail.
- Major corporations like Meta, Microsoft, and Amazon are restructuring workforces to prioritize AI, increasing the necessity for workers to master AI-driven workflows.
- By automating repetitive tasks like scheduling and data handling, AI provides female founders with more capacity to focus on leadership and strategic business growth.
- Inclusive participation in AI development ensures that future technologies reflect diverse perspectives, preventing the reinforcement of existing societal and gender-based inequalities.

### Why it Matters
The shift toward AI-driven production represents a fundamental change in who can participate in the digital economy and influence future technological systems. If women are actively encouraged to build with these tools, they can overcome historical barriers to funding and technical access, ultimately fostering a more equitable and innovative business landscape.
title: Closing the AI literacy gap is the real gender equalizer in tech
author: Cien Solon

summary:
Microsoft has identified an ongoing cryptojacking campaign using SEO poisoning and AI-generated recommendations to trick users into downloading GPU-mining malware disguised as popular PC utility software.

### Key Points
- Attackers impersonate legitimate tools like CrystalDiskInfo, HWMonitor, and FurMark to target users with high-performance graphics cards.
- The malware uses DLL sideloading to execute payloads and establishes persistence by injecting code into trusted Microsoft-signed .NET utilities.
- Malicious links have been observed appearing in AI chatbot responses, marking an emerging trend in AI-assisted search poisoning.
- Once installed, the malware deploys ScreenConnect for remote access and runs GPU miners like lolMiner, gminer, and SRBMiner-MULTI.
- The campaign has utilized over 150 malicious domains since March 2026 to evade detection and maintain long-term system access.

### Why it Matters
This campaign demonstrates how threat actors are evolving to exploit both traditional search engines and emerging AI-assisted discovery tools to distribute malware. Users must prioritize downloading software exclusively from official vendor websites to avoid sophisticated threats that bypass standard security measures.
title: Microsoft warns GPU mining malware is being spread to users through SEO poisoning and AI chatbots — cryptojacking campaign targets gamers and high-end PC users with downloads disguised as popular PC utilities
author: Etiido Uko

summary:
Kevin O’Leary is facing intense public backlash over the proposed 40,000-acre Stratos data center project in Utah, which critics argue threatens local water supplies and exacerbates environmental degradation.

### Key Points
- The Stratos project covers 40,000 acres near the drought-stricken Great Salt Lake, an area more than double the size of Manhattan.
- Policy director Jim Walsh of Food and Water Watch warns the facility will increase reliance on the Ruby Pipeline, further incentivizing water-intensive fracking.
- Data centers currently consume roughly 176 terawatt-hours of electricity annually, with projections suggesting a massive increase to 580 terawatt-hours by 2028.
- The Trump administration is actively seeking to accelerate data center development by easing federal regulations and permitting requirements.
- Local communities in states like Georgia and Utah are increasingly enacting moratoriums to address concerns over water quality, pressure, and resource depletion.

### Why it Matters
The rapid expansion of AI infrastructure is creating a direct conflict between the tech industry’s energy demands and the essential resource needs of local communities. This tension highlights a growing national debate over whether the economic and strategic benefits of data centers justify the long-term environmental costs to public water and power grids.
title: The Race to Build AI Data Centers — Before the People Can Protest
author: The Intercept Briefing

summary:
The new SwitchBot Rechargeable Button Pusher now features native Matter support, allowing users to easily integrate physical appliance buttons into the Apple Home ecosystem without complex software workarounds.

### Key Points
- The updated SwitchBot device includes a 370 mAh lithium-ion battery that provides up to six months of usage per charge.
- Users can now connect the button pusher to Apple Home by scanning a Matter pairing code via a compatible SwitchBot hub.
- The device functions as a physical robotic arm designed to press buttons on appliances that do not support smart connectivity.
- Automations can be configured in the Apple Home app to trigger the device based on motion sensor data or specific time ranges.
- The hardware features a USB-C port for recharging and replaces previous methods that required API keys and third-party plugins.

### Why it Matters
This update significantly lowers the technical barrier for users looking to automate non-smart appliances within the Apple Home framework. By adopting the Matter standard, SwitchBot simplifies the setup process, making advanced home automation more accessible to mainstream consumers.
title: HomeKit Weekly: SwitchBot Button Pusher finally works natively with Apple Home
author: Bradley C
Jon Reed
title: Anthropic Says a Mythos-Class AI Model Will Be Available Soon
summary:
Anthropic has released the upgraded Claude Opus 4.8 model while continuing to restrict public access to its powerful Mythos AI due to ongoing cybersecurity and safety testing requirements.

### Key Points
- Anthropic launched Claude Opus 4.8, which offers improved benchmark performance and reduced hallucination rates compared to the previous 4.7 version.
- The company is currently limiting access to its advanced Mythos Preview model to select partners under the Project Glasswing initiative.
- Mythos demonstrated its utility by identifying over 200 security vulnerabilities for the latest version of the Mozilla Firefox browser.
- New features on Claude.ai and Claude Cowork allow users to adjust model "effort" settings to balance response quality against usage limits.
- Cybersecurity experts note that Mythos is roughly 30 times more expensive to operate than previous models, potentially limiting its use by smaller threat actors.

### Why it Matters
The phased release of the Mythos model highlights the growing tension between rapid AI innovation and the need for robust safeguards against potential cybersecurity exploits. As these powerful tools become more accessible, the industry faces a critical window to implement defenses before the technology is widely adopted by both developers and malicious actors.

author: Amanda Goh
title: Dan Loeb says the only way to 'get good' at AI is to start using it now
summary:
Third Point founder Dan Loeb admits he underestimated the speed of AI disruption, prompting the $24 billion hedge fund to integrate computer scientists and AI tools into daily operations.

### Key Points
- Dan Loeb acknowledged that Third Point misjudged how quickly artificial intelligence would impact specific information services and proprietary business models.
- The hedge fund has hired computer scientists to coach staff and assist with internal projects to better adapt to rapid technological shifts.
- Loeb dismissed claims that AI is a market bubble, asserting that the industry has only begun to realize the technology's full potential.
- Employees at Third Point are currently utilizing AI tools like Anthropic’s Claude for tasks ranging from simple queries to running autonomous agents.
- Major corporations, including JPMorgan Chase, Disney, and Google, are increasingly tracking employee AI adoption through internal dashboards and performance reviews.

### Why it Matters
The shift toward tracking AI usage reflects a broader corporate trend where businesses treat AI proficiency as a critical metric for employee productivity and organizational success. As firms invest billions into these technologies, the pressure to demonstrate tangible returns is fundamentally changing how companies evaluate and manage their workforce.

author: Eric Song
title: Save $1,000 Off the Powerful Lenovo Legion Pro 7 RTX 5080 Gaming Laptop With 16" OLED Display
summary:
B&H Photo is currently offering significant discounts on Lenovo Legion Pro 7i and Legion 5 gaming laptops, featuring the latest Nvidia RTX 50-series graphics cards and Intel processors.

### Key Points
- The Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 10 with an RTX 5080 is discounted by $1,000, bringing the price to $2,499.
- Featured hardware includes the Intel Core Ultra 9 275HX processor, 32GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 2.5K 240Hz OLED display.
- Additional deals include savings of $950 on the RTX 5090 model and $550 on the Legion 5 RTX 5070 configuration.
- The RTX 5080 mobile GPU offers performance exceeding the previous generation's RTX 4090, particularly when utilizing DLSS 4.5 technology.
- All featured laptops include user-upgradeable RAM and SSD storage slots for future hardware expansion.

### Why it Matters
These discounts provide substantial price reductions on high-end mobile hardware equipped with the latest generation of Nvidia graphics and Intel processing power. This sale offers a timely opportunity for gamers and power users to acquire premium workstations capable of handling demanding titles and AI-driven tasks at a lower entry cost.

author: robinostlund
title: Volkswagen blocks Home Assistant by requiring client assertion
summary:
Users of the Home Assistant Volkswagen Carnet integration are reporting authentication failures that prevent login, despite the official Volkswagen mobile app and web portal remaining fully functional for them.

### Key Points
- Users report an "authentication expired" error when attempting to log in through the Home Assistant integration.
- The official Volkswagen Connect Android app and the vwgroup.io web portal continue to operate without issues.
- Affected users have confirmed their credentials are valid and that no multi-factor authentication is enabled on their accounts.
- The bug prevents the integration from connecting to vehicle data, rendering the Home Assistant dashboard unable to interact with the car.

### Why it Matters
This issue disrupts the ability of Home Assistant users to monitor and control their Volkswagen vehicles through their smart home interface. It highlights a potential compatibility break between the third-party integration and Volkswagen's authentication servers, requiring a technical update to restore service.

author: Megan Ellis
title: Your Android phone has a hidden dashboard that shows which apps are spying on you
summary:
Android’s built-in privacy dashboard provides users with a centralized, user-friendly interface to monitor and manage app permissions, helping identify which applications are accessing sensitive data like location or contacts.

### Key Points
- The privacy dashboard displays a 24-hour timeline of app permissions, including specific timestamps for camera, microphone, and location access.
- Users can navigate to the tool via Settings, Security & privacy, or by searching for "Privacy dashboard" directly in the device menu.
- Samsung Galaxy devices feature a customized version of the dashboard located within the Security and privacy settings under One UI.
- The interface allows users to instantly revoke permissions for specific apps that may be accessing data unnecessarily.
- Additional security can be maintained by running Google Play Protect scans to identify potential malware or unauthorized app behavior.

### Why it Matters
Regularly auditing app permissions is essential for maintaining digital security and preventing unauthorized data collection by third-party applications. This tool empowers users to make informed decisions about their privacy settings, reducing the risk of background tracking and unnecessary battery consumption.

author: Andy Polk
title: Glass Jaw? The New Economic Fragility Recasting American Power
summary:
Broadly applied U.S. tariffs have eroded the economic system's ability to absorb global shocks, leaving American businesses and national security strategies increasingly vulnerable to compounding crises like rising oil prices.

### Key Points
- Broad tariffs have forced companies to exhaust margins and reduce product quality, eliminating the financial buffers needed to navigate sudden supply chain disruptions.
- The 2026 Iran conflict triggered an oil price surge that, when layered onto existing tariff-induced fragility, has caused rapid, widespread inflation for consumers.
- Footwear industry data shows that 70 percent of a sneaker's cost is exposed to oil price fluctuations, illustrating how energy costs now permeate the entire supply chain.
- A 2025 KPMG survey found 43 percent of consumer goods executives reported significant declines in gross margins as they struggled to absorb rising input costs.
- The Trump administration’s reliance on Section 122 and potential Section 301 tariffs continues to create an unpredictable trade environment that discourages long-term business investment.

### Why it Matters
The loss of economic resilience acts as a hard ceiling on the United States' ability to sustain military and diplomatic coercion, as domestic financial stress limits political options. Adversaries like China are observing these vulnerabilities to calibrate their own strategies, potentially viewing a financially constrained America as less capable of responding to global geopolitical challenges.

author: Raphael Satter, Reuters
title: US troops are reportedly being targeted using location data, Pentagon says
summary:
U.S. Central Command has confirmed that adversaries are exploiting commercially available location data to track and target American military personnel stationed in active war zones across the globe.

### Key Points
- U.S. Central Command acknowledged receiving multiple reports of adversaries using commercial location data to surveil troops in theater.
- Senator Ron Wyden and a bipartisan group of lawmakers are urging the Pentagon to implement stricter data privacy protocols for military personnel.
- Lawmakers suggest disabling unique advertising IDs and restricting the use of data-heavy browsers like Google Chrome on government-issued devices.
- Commercial location data is typically harvested from smartphone apps and sold through a complex network of data brokers to advertisers.
- Previous investigations have demonstrated that this data can track special operations forces from domestic bases to sensitive international staging posts.

### Why it Matters
The use of commercial data for military targeting highlights a significant national security vulnerability created by the unregulated global adtech industry. This trend forces the Pentagon to reconsider how everyday digital habits and smartphone usage patterns can inadvertently compromise the safety of personnel in combat zones.

author: Kaitlyn Cimino
title: This new projector lineup is all about summer sports and outdoor viewing
summary:
AWOL Vision has unveiled a new lineup of ultra-short-throw projectors and inflatable outdoor screens designed to enhance home theater and backyard sports viewing experiences throughout the summer season.

### Key Points
- The Aetherion Pro ultra-short-throw projector features 4K resolution, low latency, and high refresh rates for bright-room sports and gaming.
- The Valerion Max Theater Package utilizes triple-laser technology to provide a high-end cinematic experience for home theater enthusiasts.
- New red and purple color variants for the Valerion Pro 2 projector aim to integrate the hardware into diverse lifestyle and gaming room aesthetics.
- The new Valerion inflatable outdoor screens feature wrinkle-resistant, washable fabric and a silent, sealed-air construction that sets up in 10 minutes.
- Pricing begins at $2,699 for the Valerion Pro 2, $3,499 for the Aetherion Pro, and $4,199 for the Valerion Max Theater Package.

### Why it Matters
This product expansion positions AWOL Vision to capture consumer interest ahead of major summer sporting events like the FIFA World Cup. By offering specialized outdoor hardware and lifestyle-focused designs, the company is shifting projectors from niche home theater equipment into versatile, centerpiece entertainment solutions.

author: Chris Hachey
title: 4 smart home projects you should never DIY (even if the subreddit says you can)
summary:
Homeowners looking to upgrade their smart home systems should identify which complex installations, such as electrical panels and water monitors, require professional assistance to ensure safety and efficiency.

### Key Points
- Electrical panel upgrades and modifications should be handled by licensed electricians to avoid fire hazards and ensure compliance with building codes.
- Installing a Moen Flo smart water monitor requires professional plumbing expertise because it connects directly to the home's main water supply line.
- Hardwired security systems and cameras often involve complex cable routing and drilling that may be better suited for professional installers.
- Replacing ISP-provided modems and routers with custom hardware can improve network security but may lead to cable management issues if not installed correctly.
- Wireless smart home devices, such as standalone cameras, are generally user-friendly and do not require professional installation.

### Why it Matters
Recognizing the limits of DIY home improvement is essential for preventing costly property damage, fire risks, and potential insurance complications. Hiring professionals for high-stakes infrastructure tasks ensures that smart home technology functions reliably while maintaining the long-term safety and value of the residence.

author: Andrew Romero
title: Peak Design’s small Travel Backpack is the exact tech bag I’ve been looking for
summary:
The Peak Design 20L Travel Backpack offers a minimalist, weather-resistant solution for everyday carry, balancing a compact 20-liter capacity with expandable storage for laptops and professional camera equipment.

### Key Points
- The 20L Travel Backpack features a 400D recycled nylon Versa Shell exterior with a 900D weatherproof base.
- It accommodates laptops up to 16 inches and includes C-clip loops for integrating Peak Design camera cubes.
- The bag measures 6 inches deep but expands to 8.4 inches, providing an additional 3 liters of internal volume.
- Peak Design also introduced a 3L Travel Crossbody bag featuring origami-style organization and a magnetic phone pocket for $99.95.
- The 20L backpack retails for $199, though early-bird pricing is often available through company crowdfunding campaigns.

### Why it Matters
This backpack represents a shift toward streamlined, versatile gear for professionals who prioritize mobility over carrying excess equipment. By offering a high-quality, durable option for daily use, Peak Design addresses the market demand for premium, compact bags that transition seamlessly from office environments to travel.

author: Ellsworth Toohey
title: A coder fed 20 years of his messages to AI to audit his friendships
summary:
Software engineer Vadim Drobinin analyzed two decades of his personal chat logs using large language models to create a digital archive that evaluates his historical social relationships and communication patterns.

### Key Points
- Drobinin processed 1.2 million messages from platforms including ICQ, IRC, VK, Twitter, Facebook, Instagram, and Telegram.
- Data analysis revealed that increased question-asking often correlates with thinning friendships rather than deepening connections.
- Vocabulary overlap with certain contacts dropped from 69.5% to 8.7% over time, indicating a significant shift in shared language.
- The project identified a personal tendency to offer unsolicited advice rather than active listening during conversations.
- Despite his active network shrinking from 275 to 60 contacts since 2016, his total annual conversation volume remained consistent.

### Why it Matters
This experiment demonstrates how individuals can leverage personal data access laws and generative AI to gain objective insights into their own behavioral patterns and social history. It highlights the potential for personal CRM tools to challenge subjective memories and provide a data-driven perspective on long-term interpersonal dynamics.

author: Yaz Bekkar
title: Closing the security blind spots that are a prime entry point for attacks
summary:
A new report from Barracuda highlights that most cybersecurity breaches stem from preventable vulnerabilities like unpatched firewalls, dormant accounts, and misconfigured security tools rather than sophisticated external attacks.

### Key Points
- Attackers frequently exploit "blind spots" such as forgotten third-party vendor accounts and unmanaged devices that bypass corporate security controls.
- Threat actors are increasingly using "living-off-the-land" techniques, leveraging legitimate administrative tools like PowerShell to remain undetected within a network.
- Research indicates that the time between an initial breach and a full-scale ransomware attack can be as short as three hours.
- The adoption of agentic AI by cybercriminals is accelerating the speed at which attackers can scan environments and identify configuration weaknesses.
- Barracuda recommends prioritizing consistent multi-factor authentication, disciplined patch management, and unified security visibility to mitigate these common risks.

### Why it Matters
Organizations often focus on defending against advanced threats while neglecting basic security hygiene, which creates significant operational vulnerabilities. By failing to address these fundamental gaps, businesses remain susceptible to rapid, automated attacks that can cause catastrophic damage.

author: Alina Maria Stan
title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket exploded on the launch pad, destroying its only pad and threatening Amazon’s satellite deadline
summary:
A Blue Origin New Glenn rocket exploded during a static fire test at Cape Canaveral on May 28, destroying the vehicle and critical launch pad infrastructure at the site.

### Key Points
- The explosion occurred at 9 p.m. EDT during a test of the rocket’s seven BE-4 methane-fueled engines.
- The blast destroyed the rocket, the erector-gantry, and a lightning tower at Launch Complex 36.
- No personnel were injured during the incident, which Blue Origin officially classified as an "anomaly."
- The failure jeopardizes the upcoming NG-4 mission, which was intended to carry 48 Amazon Leo broadband satellites into orbit.
- Amazon faces a strict FCC deadline to deploy 1,618 satellites by July 30, 2026, to maintain its spectrum license.
- The incident occurred one day after NASA awarded Blue Origin a $188 million contract for lunar rover deliveries.

### Why it Matters
The destruction of the only launch pad capable of supporting New Glenn creates a significant bottleneck for Amazon’s satellite internet constellation and threatens the company's regulatory compliance. This failure undermines Blue Origin's commercial credibility at a pivotal time as the firm seeks to compete with SpaceX for dominance in the global launch market.

author: Andrew Nesbitt
title: Protestware for Coding Agents
summary:
The Java testing library jqwik released version 1.10.0 containing a hidden prompt injection command designed to manipulate AI coding agents by instructing them to delete project source code.

### Key Points
- Version 1.10.0 of the jqwik testing library includes a method named `printMessageForCodingAgents` that outputs a malicious command to standard output.
- The command uses ANSI escape sequences to hide the text from human terminal users while remaining visible to automated CI logs and AI coding tools.
- The maintainer explicitly included this code as a form of "openly communicated resistance" against the use of generative AI in software development.
- Security researchers note that traditional supply-chain scanners fail to detect this behavior because the library performs no unauthorized system calls or file modifications.
- The project documentation now includes a warning that using jqwik with AI coding agents is strongly discouraged.

### Why it Matters
This incident highlights a new class of supply-chain risk where legitimate dependencies are used to deliver prompt injections directly into the context windows of AI coding assistants. Because the malicious instructions are delivered through standard output rather than traditional code execution, they bypass existing security tooling and automated provenance checks.

author: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)
title: Kimsuky Deploys HTTPSpy, Expands Arsenal with HelloDoor and VS Code Tunnels
summary:
The North Korean state-sponsored hacking group Kimsuky targeted South Korean military and corporate entities throughout March and April 2026 using sophisticated social engineering and HTTPSpy malware variants.

### Key Points
- Kimsuky impersonated security software installers and Cisco Webex meeting pages to distribute malicious payloads to South Korean organizations.
- The attacks utilized HTTPSpy, a remote access trojan capable of capturing screenshots, executing shell commands, and exfiltrating sensitive data from compromised endpoints.
- Attackers leveraged stolen meeting schedules to create convincing lures, specifically targeting messaging administrators and service members.
- New technical methods include "JSONPing" for real-time infection verification and the abuse of legitimate Microsoft VS Code tunneling for persistent remote access.
- The group continues to evolve its toolkit, incorporating Rust-based malware like HelloDoor and HttpMalice, which are reportedly developed with the assistance of large language models.

### Why it Matters
These campaigns demonstrate a significant escalation in the sophistication of North Korean cyber espionage, moving beyond simple phishing to highly tailored, context-aware attacks. By exploiting legitimate software and stolen credentials, Kimsuky poses a persistent threat to the operational security of critical defense, government, and private sector infrastructure.

author: Devony Hof
title: Three insights you might have missed from theCUBE’s coverage of Boomi World 2026
summary:
At Boomi World 2026, Boomi LP shifted its focus from generalized AI models toward enterprise-grade data activation, governance, and agentic workflows to help businesses scale real-time operations.

### Key Points
- Boomi introduced Agentstudio and Boomi Connect to provide unified governance and cost management for enterprise AI agents.
- The company announced a collaboration with Red Hat Inc. to pair Agentstudio with Red Hat AI, enabling private, containerized agentic environments.
- Boomi Companion was launched as an open-source suite allowing coding agents to build production-ready solutions using natural language.
- Lexitas, a Chronicle Bidco Inc. company, has successfully automated nearly 50% of its regulated payment processing using Boomi’s agentic platform.
- Boomi is prioritizing "liquid data" and low-latency pipelines to support the transition toward a "headless enterprise" driven by agent-to-agent communication.

### Why it Matters
By shifting focus from model hype to infrastructure, Boomi is addressing the critical enterprise need for secure, domain-specific AI that delivers measurable return on investment. This approach allows organizations to integrate autonomous agents into business processes while maintaining strict control over data privacy and operational governance.

author: Yaroslav Shmelev, Anton Kivva, Denis Parinov, Vladimir Kuskov, Yanina Balandyuk-Opalinskaya
title: What’s in the container? Analyzing vulnerabilities, risks and protection with Kaspersky Container Security and the KIRA AI assistant
summary:
Kaspersky researchers warn that most popular Docker Hub images contain critical vulnerabilities and insecure configurations, necessitating automated security scanning to prevent container-based cyberattacks and enterprise network compromises.

### Key Points
- A study of 100 popular Docker images revealed that 64% contain critical software vulnerabilities, while only 10% are fully updated.
- Common security flaws include hardcoded plaintext passwords, excessive sudo privileges, and insecure file permissions like 777.
- Attackers frequently exploit outdated software, such as CVE-2025-49844 in Redis or CVE-2026-24061 in Nginx, to gain remote code execution.
- Kaspersky introduced an AI assistant named KIRA within its Container Security platform to identify misconfigurations and provide remediation guidance.
- Supply chain risks remain high, as infrequent updates leave systems vulnerable, while frequent updates can introduce malicious dependencies if not properly scanned.

### Why it Matters
Containerized infrastructure has become a primary target for attackers seeking to deploy malware, steal data, or move laterally through enterprise networks. Implementing automated security audits and robust secret management is essential to mitigate the risks posed by vulnerable third-party images and configuration errors.

author: jez@windowscentral.com (Jez Corden) , Jez Corden
title: Xbox CEO Asha Sharma warns tough decisions are ahead, raising questions about Helix, Xbox Game Studios, and exclusives
summary:
Xbox CEO Asha Sharma has signaled that the company faces difficult strategic decisions to improve profitability and address shifting market conditions ahead of the upcoming June 7 Showcase.

### Key Points
- CEO Asha Sharma announced a shift toward a more deliberate brand strategy, citing the need for hard choices regarding investments and future company structure.
- Microsoft remains committed to producing Xbox hardware despite supply chain challenges and high costs associated with sourcing memory components.
- Recent price cuts for Xbox Game Pass Ultimate have reportedly begun to reverse negative subscription and retention trends.
- The company is evaluating the fiscal performance of smaller Xbox Game Studios acquisitions to improve overall margins.
- Analysts anticipate potential price increases for Xbox Series X|S consoles later this year as existing memory supply contracts expire.

### Why it Matters
These strategic shifts reflect broader economic pressures facing the consumer tech industry, including rising hardware costs and increased competition for user attention. How Microsoft balances these fiscal demands with fan expectations for exclusive content will determine the long-term viability of the Xbox platform.

author: Scott Webster
title: XREAL Launches New X By XREAL Brand and Debuts a01 AR Glasses Starting at $299
summary:
XREAL has launched a new sub-brand called X By XREAL and introduced the a01, a lightweight pair of augmented reality glasses designed for accessible, everyday media and gaming consumption.

### Key Points
- The a01 glasses weigh 62 grams and feature an interchangeable front-frame system that supports 3D-printed customization.
- The device utilizes dual-layer MicroOLED displays with 1600-nit brightness and HDR10 support to simulate a 147-inch screen.
- Integrated spatial anti-shake technology is designed to stabilize images for users during travel on trains, subways, or flights.
- The product includes AI-powered SDR-to-HDR conversion to enhance contrast and color presentation for streaming and gaming.
- The a01 is currently available in China and is scheduled for a United States release in July with a $299 starting price.

### Why it Matters
This launch represents a strategic shift for XREAL to capture the mass-market consumer segment by prioritizing affordability and personal style over high-end professional specifications. By lowering the barrier to entry, the company aims to normalize augmented reality hardware as a standard accessory for mobile entertainment and daily commuting.

author: Marcus Mendes
title: Apple says latest conflict minerals review found no basis to link suppliers to armed groups
summary:
Apple has submitted its annual conflict minerals disclosure to the SEC, confirming that its supply chain for products like iPhone and Mac avoids financing armed groups in the Congo.

### Key Points
- Apple’s report covers the 2025 calendar year and includes all major hardware, such as iPhone, Mac, iPad, Apple Vision Pro, and various accessories.
- The company mandates that 100% of identified smelters and refiners for tin, tantalum, tungsten, and gold (3TG) undergo annual independent third-party audits.
- Apple states it found no reasonable basis to conclude that its supply chain smelters or refiners directly or indirectly financed armed groups in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.
- The company maintains a policy to terminate business relationships with any suppliers or smelters that refuse to meet its responsible sourcing and audit standards.
- Apple acknowledges that it cannot always verify the specific country of origin for minerals due to its reliance on supplier-provided data rather than direct mine-site procurement.

### Why it Matters
This disclosure is a critical component of Apple's regulatory compliance and corporate social responsibility efforts regarding human rights in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. By maintaining these transparency standards, the company attempts to mitigate legal risks and reputational damage associated with allegations of forced labor and unethical mineral sourcing in the global tech supply chain.

Amazon has officially ended support for Kindle models released before 2012, preventing these devices from connecting to the Kindle Store to purchase or download new digital book content.

### Key Points
- Affected devices include the original Kindle, Kindle Keyboard, Kindle Touch, Kindle 4, Kindle 5, first-generation Kindle Paperwhite, and early Kindle Fire tablets.
- Users can still read books already downloaded to their devices, provided they do not perform a factory reset or deregister the account.
- Owners can continue adding content by sideloading DRM-free EPUB or PDF files via a USB cable or by using management software like Calibre.
- Jailbreaking is a technical alternative for advanced users to install open-source viewers, though it carries a high risk of permanently damaging the hardware.

### Why it Matters
This policy change highlights the limitations of hardware longevity in an ecosystem reliant on cloud-based digital storefronts. While users can extend the life of their devices through manual file management, the loss of direct store access serves as a practical prompt for consumers to consider upgrading to modern, supported eReaders.
author: Christine Persaud
title: How to sideload books on your Kindle after Amazon cuts off the store

summary:
Chinese robotics startup LinkerBot has emerged as a dominant market leader by manufacturing affordable, dexterous robotic hands that are currently being adopted by global manufacturers and research laboratories.

### Key Points
- LinkerBot produces robotic hands with at least 11 joints, priced as low as $600, with plans to reduce costs to $200 within five years.
- The company shipped 10,000 units last year, capturing approximately 80 percent of the global market demand for dexterous robotic hands.
- Founded in 2023, the startup is currently seeking a new funding round at a $6 billion valuation and is reportedly exploring a Hong Kong IPO.
- Investors in the company include Ant Group, HongShan Capital, and the Chinese government.
- LinkerBot’s hands are capable of complex tasks including playing piano, threading needles, and assembling electronics.

### Why it Matters
By focusing exclusively on the most difficult component of humanoid robotics, LinkerBot is positioning itself as a critical supplier for the broader automation industry. This strategy allows the company to scale rapidly while helping other manufacturers lower costs and accelerate the global adoption of humanoid robots.
author: Zeyi Yang
title: The $6 Billion Chinese Startup Trying to Build Hands for Every Robot

summary:
Google is introducing a Gemini-powered automation feature for Google Home that allows Nest and select third-party cameras to trigger smart home routines based on visual object recognition.

### Key Points
- Users can create custom automations by describing specific visual events, such as detecting mail deliveries or animals near trash bins.
- The feature requires a Google Home Premium Advanced subscription, costing $20 per month or $200 annually.
- It is currently available in English for US users enrolled in the Google Home Public Preview program.
- Gemini for Home now supports complex, multi-action voice commands and offers improved response times for natural language requests.
- Google advises against using these visual automations for time-sensitive security or safety purposes due to processing latency.

### Why it Matters
This update marks a significant shift toward context-aware smart homes that react to environmental changes rather than just manual inputs. By integrating generative AI with home security hardware, Google is positioning its ecosystem to offer more personalized and intuitive automation for premium subscribers.
author: Jess Weatherbed
title: Gemini for Google Home can now use your cameras to trigger automations

summary:
The Canadian government is amending the controversial Bill C-22 to clarify encryption and metadata rules following significant backlash from major technology companies and privacy-focused service providers regarding digital security.

### Key Points
- Public Safety Minister Gary Anandasangaree confirmed the government is drafting amendments to better define encryption and metadata requirements within the proposed legislation.
- Tech giants including Apple, Google, and Meta warned that the original bill could weaken global cybersecurity and facilitate foreign interference.
- Several VPN providers, such as Proton VPN, ExpressVPN, and NordVPN, threatened to exit the Canadian market if forced to compromise their no-logs policies.
- The original bill would have required electronic service providers to retain metadata for up to one year and comply with secret data-retrieval orders.
- Despite the revisions, the government maintains that updated lawful access tools are necessary for intelligence agencies to combat modern digital threats.

### Why it Matters
The outcome of this legislation will set a critical precedent for the balance between national security surveillance and the protection of user privacy in the digital age. If passed, the bill could force major global tech firms to choose between complying with Canadian law or withdrawing their services from the country entirely.
author: Rene Millman
title: Canada vows to amend Bill C-22's encryption and metadata rules amid massive tech backlash

summary:
Anthropic has launched Claude Opus 4.8, an upgraded AI model featuring improved reasoning, faster performance, and new dynamic workflow capabilities to handle complex, large-scale tasks more effectively for users.

### Key Points
- Claude Opus 4.8 improves agentic coding scores to 69.2% and increases knowledge work performance to 1890.
- The model’s fast mode is now 2.5 times quicker and three times more cost-effective than the previous version.
- New features include dynamic workflows for large-scale tasks and effort control settings for claude.ai and Cowork.
- The Messages API now supports system entries within the messages array to allow mid-task instruction updates.
- Anthropic plans to release its specialized Mythos-class cybersecurity model to all customers in the coming weeks.

### Why it Matters
This rapid release cycle demonstrates Anthropic's commitment to maintaining a competitive edge in the enterprise AI market by prioritizing agentic performance and operational efficiency. These updates provide developers and businesses with more granular control over model effort and cost, facilitating the integration of AI into complex, long-running professional workflows.
author: Zac Hall
title: Anthropic upgrades Claude with new Opus 4.8 model, details here

summary:
Threat actors are exploiting a critical pre-authentication vulnerability in FortiClient Endpoint Management Server to deploy credential-stealing malware by masquerading malicious payloads as legitimate software updates across managed endpoints.

### Key Points
- Cybersecurity firm Arctic Wolf identified the exploitation of CVE-2026-35616, a critical vulnerability with a CVSS score of 9.1.
- Attackers bypass API authentication to modify EMS configurations and push malicious PowerShell scripts to all managed endpoint devices.
- The campaign uses a fake update file, "FortiEndpoint_Patch.exe," to harvest sensitive browser data including passwords, cookies, and credit card information.
- Fortinet addressed the security flaw in FortiClient EMS version 7.4.7 and subsequent releases.
- Stolen data is exfiltrated to an attacker-controlled server at 83.138.53.110 via HTTP POST requests.

### Why it Matters
This vulnerability allows attackers to weaponize trusted management infrastructure, turning a single server compromise into a widespread breach of all connected endpoints. The theft of session cookies and credentials poses a significant risk, as these can be used to bypass multi-factor authentication and gain unauthorized access to sensitive cloud and internal applications.
author: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)
title: Threat Actors Exploit Critical FortiClient EMS Flaw to Deploy Credential Stealer

summary:
GlobalFoundries, UMC, and SMIC are navigating distinct strategic paths in the trailing-edge semiconductor market, balancing massive capital investments against shifting geopolitical regulations and rising demand for specialized chips.

### Key Points
- GlobalFoundries reported $6.79 billion in 2025 revenue, focusing on specialty platforms like 22FDX and silicon photonics while leveraging U.S. CHIPS Act funding for domestic expansion.
- UMC achieved a 107.9% year-over-year net income surge and is partnering with Intel to develop a 12nm FinFET node in Arizona, targeting mass production by 2027.
- SMIC posted $9.33 billion in 2025 revenue, aggressively expanding mature-node capacity despite U.S. export controls that limit its access to advanced lithography equipment.
- The mature-node foundry sector is seeing a price floor as TSMC reallocates capacity toward AI-related advanced packaging, tightening supply for automotive and power management chips.
- Combined, these three foundries hold approximately 13.5% of the global market, with each firm pursuing different geographic and technological strategies to maintain competitiveness.

### Why it Matters
These foundries provide the essential components for critical infrastructure, including automotive systems, power supplies for AI servers, and defense technology. Their ability to scale production and navigate international trade restrictions directly impacts the stability and cost of the global electronics supply chain.
author: Luke James
title: Trailing-edge foundry roadmaps for GlobalFoundries, UMC, and SMIC — mature node chipmakers each pursue differing strategies and IP

summary:
The Trump administration has mandated that most visa holders must return to their home countries to apply for green cards, significantly altering long-standing U.S. immigration adjustment procedures.

### Key Points
- USCIS issued a memo requiring applicants to apply from abroad, except in cases deemed to involve "extraordinary circumstances."
- The policy threatens the residency status of hundreds of thousands of skilled workers, including physicians and Silicon Valley technology professionals.
- Current immigration law often forces applicants from countries like India and China to wait over a decade for green card approval.
- The directive marks a victory for hardline nativist factions within the MAGA movement over tech-industry leaders who advocated for merit-based immigration.
- Implementation remains ambiguous, as the memo grants USCIS officers significant discretion in determining which applicants qualify for exceptions.

### Why it Matters
This policy shift creates substantial uncertainty for the U.S. technology sector, which relies heavily on foreign-born STEM talent to maintain global competitiveness. By deterring highly skilled workers from seeking permanent residency, the administration risks a "brain drain" that could drive international talent toward other nations and disrupt domestic labor markets.
author: Eric Levitz
title: MAGA’s civil war over immigration is over. Silicon Valley lost.

summary:
VanEck has officially launched the first U.S. spot BNB exchange-traded fund on the Nasdaq, providing investors with regulated exposure to the native token of the BNB Chain ecosystem.

### Key Points
- The VanEck BNB ETF trades under the ticker VBNB and carries a 0.39% sponsor fee.
- Shares are fully backed by BNB tokens held in cold storage by custodian Anchorage Digital Bank.
- BNB Chain currently processes over 14 million daily transactions and supports 2.5 million active users.
- The network holds more than $16 billion in stablecoins and $3.6 billion in tokenized real-world assets.
- This launch follows the successful introduction of spot bitcoin and ether ETFs in the U.S. market.

### Why it Matters
This product allows institutional and retail investors to gain exposure to BNB through traditional brokerage accounts without the technical complexities of managing digital wallets. It signals a continued expansion of crypto-based financial products in the U.S. following the established success of bitcoin and ether ETFs.
author: Francisco Rodrigues
title: VanEck launches first U.S. spot BNB ETF on Nasdaq

summary:
The U.S. smartphone market declined by 3% year-over-year in Q1 2026, with Apple maintaining a 60% market share despite broader industry challenges and rising component costs.

### Key Points
- Total U.S. smartphone shipments reached 33.4 million units during the first quarter of 2026.
- Apple led the market with a 60% share, while Samsung followed with 24% after a 5% year-over-year decline.
- Motorola was the only major manufacturer to achieve growth, recording an 18% increase driven by its refreshed Moto G lineup.
- Market contraction was attributed to restrained carrier upgrade cycles, increased memory and storage costs, and delayed premium device launches.
- Entry-level devices priced under $300 saw an 8% increase, contrasting with a 1% decline in the premium sector.
- Omdia projects the overall U.S. smartphone market will experience a 4% decline for the full year of 2026.

### Why it Matters
The shift toward entry-level devices suggests that consumers are becoming more price-sensitive amid rising hardware costs and limited carrier incentives. This trend forces manufacturers to balance premium innovation with affordable options to maintain market share in a cooling economic environment.
author: nickodiaz@sbcglobal.net (Nickolas Diaz) , Nickolas Diaz
title: The US phone market struggled in Q1, but one Android maker made serious headway

summary:
Scammers are exploiting a legitimate Microsoft email address to send sophisticated phishing messages, prompting an active investigation by the tech giant to secure its automated notification systems.

### Key Points
- Attackers are utilizing the official "msonlineservicesteam@microsoftonline.com" address to send fraudulent emails to unsuspecting users.
- The compromised account is typically used by Microsoft to deliver legitimate two-factor authentication codes and security alerts.
- Evidence suggests scammers created new Microsoft accounts to bypass security filters rather than spoofing the sender's address.
- Microsoft is currently investigating the breach and removing accounts that violate its terms of service to prevent further phishing attempts.
- Users are advised to verify links and remain cautious of suspicious subject lines that mimic official transaction or security notifications.

### Why it Matters
This incident highlights a significant vulnerability in automated communication systems that users have been trained to trust for security alerts. If left unaddressed, such exploits could undermine the reliability of two-factor authentication and lead to widespread credential theft across the Microsoft ecosystem.
author: kevinokemwa@outlook.com (Kevin Okemwa) , Kevin Okemwa
title: Users report phishing emails coming from Microsoft’s system, and the company is digging in

summary:
NVIDIA faces potential market volatility due to high valuation and concentration risks, while Berkshire Hathaway offers a defensive alternative with strong cash flow and a fortress balance sheet.

### Key Points
- NVIDIA reported $81.61 billion in revenue and a $5.14 trillion market cap, with 50% of data center revenue tied to four major hyperscalers.
- NVIDIA’s supply commitments have reached $119.0 billion, while the stock trades at a trailing P/E of 33 and a price-to-book ratio of 27.
- Berkshire Hathaway trades at a P/E of 10 and a price-to-book ratio of 0.94, offering a significant valuation discount compared to current tech sector leaders.
- Berkshire maintains a low debt-to-equity ratio of 0.19 and benefits from high interest income on its substantial holdings of U.S. Treasury bills.
- Insiders including Vice Chairman Greg Abel and Warren Buffett made significant share purchases in early 2026, signaling confidence in the conglomerate's long-term value.

### Why it Matters
The contrast between NVIDIA and Berkshire Hathaway highlights a growing divide between high-growth AI-focused investments and traditional value-oriented assets. As macroeconomic conditions shift toward higher capital costs, investors are increasingly weighing the risks of concentrated tech exposure against the stability of companies with durable, cash-generative business models.
author: Alex Sirois
title: Forget Nvidia As a $39 Trillion Debt Time Bomb Meets a Divided Fed, This Unstoppable Monopoly Is a No-Brainer Buy

summary:
CNBC has appointed former The Information editor Ken Brown as its new managing editor of digital and editorial strategy to oversee digital operations and enhance cross-platform journalism.

### Key Points
- Ken Brown will lead CNBC Digital starting June 8, reporting to Editor in Chief David Cho.
- Brown previously held leadership roles at The Wall Street Journal and most recently covered AI financing at The Information.
- The appointment aims to sharpen CNBC’s coverage of business, markets, and the economic impact of emerging technologies.
- Brown is tasked with integrating digital reporting more closely with the network's television editorial leadership.
- His background includes extensive experience in investigative reporting, financial crisis coverage, and subscription-based business models.

### Why it Matters
This strategic hire signals CNBC's intent to strengthen its digital presence and compete more aggressively for audiences interested in complex financial and technological reporting. By bridging the gap between television and digital platforms, the network aims to leverage its existing brand authority to drive growth in an increasingly digital-first media landscape.
author: Ted Johnson
title: The Information’s Ken Brown To Join CNBC As Managing Editor of Digital And Editorial Strategy

summary:
Qualcomm is introducing the new Snapdragon C processor to power budget-friendly laptops, with Acer’s upcoming Aspire Go 15 serving as one of the first devices to feature the chip.

### Key Points
- The Snapdragon C processor is designed for entry-level laptops targeting a price point of approximately $300.
- Acer is the first partner to announce a device using the chip, the 15-inch Aspire Go 15, with HP and Lenovo expected to follow.
- The Aspire Go 15 features a 512GB SSD, a 1080p display, and a 1080p webcam housed in a plastic chassis.
- The device is limited to a maximum of 8GB of RAM, raising concerns about long-term performance for Windows-based productivity tasks.

### Why it Matters
This processor launch represents Qualcomm's attempt to capture the low-cost laptop market by offering a more affordable alternative to its high-performance Snapdragon X series. However, the reliance on minimal memory configurations may limit the practical utility of these devices for modern computing needs.
author: Lori Grunin
title: Qualcomm's New Snapdragon C Processors Herald an Old Spin on Cheap Laptops

summary:
23andMe faces a new lawsuit from the California Attorney General regarding a 2023 data breach, alleging the company downplayed the incident while paying a ransom to the attackers.

### Key Points
- The California Attorney General filed a lawsuit against 23andMe following a massive 2023 data breach.
- The legal action claims the genetics company failed to adequately protect sensitive user information.
- Prosecutors allege 23andMe downplayed the severity of the leak while secretly paying a ransom to hackers.
- The breach exposed the genetic data of millions of customers, raising significant privacy and security concerns.

### Why it Matters
This lawsuit highlights the growing legal and regulatory scrutiny surrounding how biotechnology firms handle sensitive genetic information. It serves as a critical reminder for companies that transparency and robust cybersecurity protocols are essential to maintaining consumer trust and avoiding severe legal consequences.
author: Connor Jones
title: Carnival confirms ShinyHunters cruised off with 6M customer records after April breach
You Should Not Update Your Dependencies
author: Olivier Gambier
summary:
Modern software development faces a critical security crisis as automated dependency updates and AI-generated code have rendered traditional manual review processes and standard package management tools dangerously obsolete.

### Key Points
- Supply chain attacks have surged because developers blindly trust and automatically merge dependency updates without verifying the underlying code.
- The rise of AI-driven coding agents has accelerated development velocity, effectively bypassing the last remaining human safety guardrail: the code review.
- Standard practices like using "latest" tags, unpinned versions, and automated tools like Dependabot are now considered primary vectors for malicious code injection.
