- Screen Reactions allow users to record their screen while simultaneously capturing selfie video in a picture-in-picture format.
- Updates extend to the broader Google ecosystem, including enhancements for Wear OS, Android Auto, smart glasses, and the Googlebook.
- The update includes long-awaited compatibility features designed to improve interoperability with rival mobile platforms like AirDrop.

### Why it Matters
These updates represent a strategic effort by Google to close the feature gap with competitors while deepening the integration across its hardware and software ecosystem. By prioritizing cross-device functionality and social media performance, Google aims to increase user retention and strengthen the appeal of its proprietary AI-driven services.
author: Peter
title: Weekly poll: which Android 17 features are you excited about?

summary:
UiPath and Deloitte have expanded their strategic partnership to integrate advanced agentic AI tools into the Ascend platform, aiming to automate software testing and accelerate global enterprise deployment cycles.

### Key Points
- UiPath is integrating its Test Cloud, Autopilot for Testers, and Agent Builder into Deloitte’s Ascend engineering platform.
- The collaboration utilizes agentic AI to autonomously generate, execute, and maintain software tests alongside human testers.
- The solution is designed to identify root causes of system failures and analyze large volumes of test data automatically.
- This integration aims to reduce manual testing tasks, improve overall test coverage, and increase the return on investment for automation.

### Why it Matters
This partnership highlights the growing industry shift toward autonomous software quality assurance driven by agentic AI. By streamlining development workflows, the integration helps global organizations reduce release times and operational costs while scaling their automation infrastructure.
author: Maham Fatima
title: UiPath (PATH), Deloitte Expand Collaboration to Integrate Test Cloud Into Ascend Platform

summary:
Robert Wright’s upcoming book, Artificial Intelligence and Our Coming Cosmic Reckoning, explores the necessity of a fundamental human reorientation to navigate the potential risks and benefits of artificial intelligence.

### Key Points
- The book, scheduled for release on June 23, argues that current AI debates between accelerationists and doomers lack a necessary cosmic perspective.
- Wright contends that humanity must shift its understanding of AI toward a more constructive "awe spectrum" to ensure positive long-term outcomes.
- The author suggests that a major transformation in human thought is required, though he notes that few people are currently prepared for such a shift.
- Critics suggest the coming transformation may be driven by status-seeking behavior rather than the philosophical or cosmic goals publicly claimed by industry leaders.

### Why it Matters
This work challenges the current discourse surrounding AI by questioning whether modern society possesses the capacity to manage grand technological visions as effectively as historical movements like the Enlightenment. Understanding these shifts is critical for stakeholders as they navigate the potential societal restructuring and power dynamics inherent in the rapid development of artificial intelligence.
author: Tyler Cowen
title: Robert Wright’s *The God Test*

summary:
Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton has filed a lawsuit against Meta, alleging the company misled millions of WhatsApp users by falsely claiming their private messages are fully end-to-end encrypted.

### Key Points
- Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton accuses Meta of misrepresenting the privacy and security protections of WhatsApp to consumers.
- The lawsuit claims Meta employees and third parties can access supposedly private communications, contradicting the company's public marketing materials.
- Meta spokesperson Andy Stone denied the allegations, stating that the company cannot access encrypted user communications and will contest the lawsuit.
- The legal action follows previous whistleblower reports and ongoing investigations by U.S. federal authorities into Meta’s data handling practices.
- Telegram founder Pavel Durov publicly criticized WhatsApp’s security, citing alleged vulnerabilities and past comments from WhatsApp co-founder Brian Acton.

### Why it Matters
This lawsuit challenges the core privacy marketing of one of the world's most popular messaging platforms, potentially impacting user trust and regulatory scrutiny. If proven, these allegations could force significant changes to Meta’s data policies and result in substantial legal penalties for the company.
author: RT
title: Meta sued for ‘misleading’ Texans over WhatsApp privacy

summary:
DeepSeek has permanently reduced the pricing for its V4 Pro API by 75%, establishing new industry benchmarks for input and output token costs to attract more enterprise developers.

### Key Points
- DeepSeek lowered V4 Pro API costs to $0.435 per 1 million input tokens and $0.87 per 1 million output tokens.
- The price reduction was originally a temporary promotion scheduled to expire on May 31 but has now been made permanent.
- This aggressive pricing strategy aims to increase market share and encourage developers to build high-volume, agentic AI applications.
- The move forces competing API providers to reevaluate their own pricing structures to remain competitive in the rapidly evolving AI model market.

### Why it Matters
Lowering per-token costs reduces financial barriers for companies building complex, high-context AI applications that require significant inference usage. By resetting these economic expectations, DeepSeek is pressuring other model providers to prioritize cost-efficiency as a primary competitive differentiator.
author: AllToc
title: DeepSeek V4 Pro API prices cut 75% #tech

summary:
The Indian Premier League is exploring the integration of blockchain-based fan tokens to deepen digital engagement and offer exclusive rewards to its massive, internet-native global cricket audience.

### Key Points
- The IPL ecosystem reached a valuation of $18.5 billion in 2025, with the league’s standalone brand value estimated at $3.9 billion.
- Current viewership for the 2025 season has surpassed 1.1 billion people across combined television and digital streaming platforms.
- Fan tokens, similar to those used by European clubs like FC Barcelona and Paris Saint-Germain, function as digital membership passes for exclusive experiences.
- Potential benefits for fans include early ticket access, signed merchandise, behind-the-scenes content, and participation in non-strategic franchise decisions.
- India’s mobile-first, gamified digital culture provides a unique infrastructure for adopting blockchain-based loyalty programs compared to traditional European models.

### Why it Matters
Fan tokens represent a significant evolution in how sports franchises monetize and sustain engagement beyond the two-month tournament window. By leveraging blockchain to provide tangible rewards, IPL teams can transform passive viewers into active, long-term community members while avoiding the pitfalls of over-financialization.
author: ET CONTRIBUTORS
title: Cricket fandom is already digital. Fan tokens could be the next mov

summary:
IMG is concluding its 22-year partnership with the Premier League as the organization transitions to an in-house production model for its global broadcast coverage starting in 2026.

### Key Points
- IMG-owned Premier League Productions (PLP) has managed global broadcast content for the league since 2004.
- The Premier League will launch its own production arm, Premier League Studios, beginning with the 2026/27 season.
- IMG will maintain a working relationship with the league regarding distribution and archive management.
- PLP currently produces content for international licensees including Sky, Prime Video, TNT Sports, Canal+, DAZN, and JioHotstar.
- The transition follows a vote by the 20 Premier League clubs to gain greater control over content and future direct-to-consumer options.

### Why it Matters
This shift marks a significant change in sports media as the Premier League moves to internalize production to capture more value and operational control. While IMG loses a major contract, the company is pivoting its resources toward other global sports properties and new technical initiatives to maintain its market position.
author: Jesse Whittock
title: Era Ends For IMG Today As Premier League Takes Production In-House: “It Was Inevitable They Would Want It”

summary:
Equity mutual funds saw strong weekly gains, with the Invesco India Infrastructure Fund leading the market by delivering a 5.70% return amid broad performance across several thematic sectors.

### Key Points
- Invesco India Infrastructure Fund achieved the highest weekly return at 5.70%.
- Nippon India Taiwan Equity Fund recorded a 4.36% gain during the same period.
- Samco Active Momentum Fund and Union Active Momentum Fund returned 3.66% and 3.08% respectively.
- Motilal Oswal Digital India Fund posted a 3.35% return, highlighting growth in the technology sector.
- Mirae Asset’s international funds focused on electric vehicles and artificial intelligence returned between 3.08% and 3.25%.

### Why it Matters
These returns highlight a period of significant growth for thematic and international equity funds, reflecting investor confidence in infrastructure, technology, and global innovation sectors. This performance underscores the potential for sector-specific strategies to outperform broader market benchmarks during periods of high volatility.
author: IANS
title: Equity MFs gain over 5% last week with international funds leading. Check top 7 with over 3% return

summary:
NEAR Protocol’s native token surged 50% to a six-month high of $2.34, leading a broader rally in AI-themed cryptocurrencies amid increased trading volumes and positive market sentiment.

### Key Points
- NEAR Protocol reached a $2.34 valuation, marking a 115% gain over the last 90 days and daily trading volumes exceeding $1 billion.
- BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes publicly endorsed the token, categorizing it as part of his "holy trinity" of altcoins alongside HYPE and ZEC.
- The upcoming v2.13 network upgrade, scheduled for June 2026, will introduce dynamic resharding and post-quantum cryptographic security features.
- The protocol is expanding its AI-focused infrastructure by developing confidential treasuries and privacy tools specifically for autonomous AI agents.
- Increased investor interest has driven higher inflows into related financial products, such as the Bitwise Near Staking ETP.

### Why it Matters
This rally highlights the growing market correlation between AI-sector developments and blockchain infrastructure projects as investors seek exposure to decentralized artificial intelligence. The protocol's focus on scaling and security upgrades positions it as a significant player for institutional capital looking to integrate AI agents with blockchain-based financial systems.
author: Editorial Team
title: NEAR Protocol surges 50% in 7 days, leads AI token rally

summary:
The Huntsville Center for Technology in Alabama is addressing the national skilled trades shortage by partnering with Toyota to train students for high-paying industrial maintenance and manufacturing careers.

### Key Points
- The $40 million Huntsville Center for Technology facility serves 700 students through industry-standard vocational training programs.
- Toyota Alabama invested $1 million to develop the "Inditech" program, which specifically targets the local need for industrial maintenance workers.
- The National Association of Manufacturers estimates the U.S. will require 1.9 million new manufacturing workers by 2033 to avoid significant economic losses.
- Data from Skillit indicates that construction workers in the booming data center sector can earn average annual salaries of approximately $81,800.
- The Alabama School of Cyber Technology and Engineering utilizes similar partnerships with companies like Raytheon and Airbus to prepare students for defense and aerospace roles.

### Why it Matters
The growing shortage of skilled tradespeople threatens to cost the U.S. economy $1 trillion annually while hindering critical infrastructure and defense production. By shifting focus toward vocational training, these public-private partnerships offer a viable path to financial stability for Gen Z workers while addressing the nation's urgent labor gaps.
author: Jake Angelo
title: As AI wipes out white-collar jobs, one Alabama high school and Toyota are training students for roles that pay $40 an hour and can’t be automated

author: Omkar Godbole
summary:
Bitcoin prices dropped to $74,300 as U.S.-listed spot exchange-traded funds recorded $2.26 billion in outflows over two weeks amid rising global bond yields and shifting investor risk appetite.

### Key Points
- Bitcoin fell to $74,305 on Saturday, marking a 10% decline from its May 6 peak of over $82,500.
- U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs experienced $1.26 billion in outflows this week, the largest weekly redemption since January.
- Rising U.S. Treasury and global bond yields have reduced market demand for non-yielding assets like cryptocurrency.
- Speculative capital is shifting toward commodities like oil and copper, as well as pre-IPO derivatives tied to SpaceX.
- Bitcoin prices recovered slightly following President Trump’s announcement of a peace agreement involving Iran and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

### Why it Matters
The significant capital flight from Bitcoin ETFs highlights how macroeconomic factors like rising bond yields can rapidly diminish investor appetite for high-risk digital assets. This shift underscores a broader market rotation as traders reallocate funds toward commodities and speculative pre-IPO opportunities amid geopolitical uncertainty.
title: Bitcoin tanks to $74,300 as spot ETFs bleed $2.26 billion in two weeks

author: Lucia Moses
summary:
Lightspeed Venture Partners has hired investor and social media creator Zau to lead seed investments and spearhead a new media strategy, including hosting the firm's upcoming AI podcast.

### Key Points
- Lightspeed Venture Partners hired Zau to bridge the gap between traditional venture capital investing and digital content creation.
- Zau brings a following of over 350,000 social media users to the firm, where she will cohost the "Lightwork" podcast.
- The role involves a dual focus on seed-stage investing and managing the Lightspeed Launch program for new founders.
- The firm aims to leverage Zau’s audience to build trust with early-stage founders and increase visibility for portfolio companies.
- This move follows a broader industry trend of venture firms, including Andreessen Horowitz and OpenAI, acquiring or developing in-house media channels.

### Why it Matters
Venture capital firms are increasingly adopting creator-led marketing strategies to secure early access to promising startups and control their own industry narratives. By integrating influencers into their investment teams, these firms hope to bypass traditional media gatekeepers and build direct, trust-based relationships with the next generation of founders.
title: There's a new type of VC in Silicon Valley: the creator-investor

author: Matt Burgess, Dell Cameron, Andrew Couts
summary:
The FBI is seeking nationwide, near real-time access to automated license plate reader data, even as US lawmakers introduce bipartisan legislation to restrict the use of such surveillance technology.

### Key Points
- The FBI Directorate of Intelligence is planning to spend millions to access searchable databases containing vehicle locations, timestamps, and license plate information.
- Google accidentally published a proof-of-concept for a high-severity Chromium vulnerability that allows websites to monitor browsing activity and launch DDoS attacks.
- The Federal Trade Commission warned 12 "nudifying" websites to implement removal processes for nonconsensual AI-generated images under the Take It Down Act.
- Two men were arrested by the Department of Justice for distributing thousands of AI-generated sexual images of real women on pornography platforms.
- Former federal prosecutor Carmen Mercedes Lineberger was charged with stealing a sealed report regarding Donald Trump’s handling of classified documents.

### Why it Matters
These developments highlight a growing tension between federal surveillance ambitions and legislative efforts to protect individual privacy and digital security. As government agencies expand their data-gathering capabilities, the public faces increasing risks from both state-sponsored tracking and unpatched software vulnerabilities.
title: The FBI Wants ‘Near Real-Time’ Access to US License Plate Readers

author: Jeremy Phillips
summary:
SpaceX disclosures reveal that the U.S. government accounted for 20.9% of the company's 2025 revenue, highlighting significant financial concentration risks ahead of its planned June 2026 Nasdaq debut.

### Key Points
- SpaceX reported $18,674 million in total revenue for 2025, with federal agencies contributing over one-fifth of that total.
- Government revenue dependency has remained high, recorded at 24.2% in 2024 and 25.2% in 2023.
- The company maintains a $28,377 million contract backlog, though no other single customer accounts for more than 10% of revenue.
- SpaceX risk disclosures warn that federal contracts can be terminated for convenience or revoked due to shifting political priorities and budget constraints.
- Compliance with Federal Acquisition Regulation and DFARS remains a critical requirement to avoid payment suspensions or contract debarment.

### Why it Matters
This heavy reliance on federal spending exposes SpaceX to significant volatility linked to U.S. political shifts and fiscal policy changes. Investors must weigh the company's growth potential against the reality that a substantial portion of its revenue stream remains subject to the unpredictable nature of government budget cycles.
title: One Number From SPACEX Pre-IPO Disclosures Reveals Just How Fragile This Is...

author: derrek.lee@futurenet.com (Derrek Lee) , Derrek Lee
summary:
Google and Samsung are collaborating to develop Android XR smart glasses, aiming to challenge Meta’s market dominance by prioritizing stylish, consumer-friendly designs over complex artificial intelligence features.

### Key Points
- Google and Samsung are partnering to create "intelligent eyewear" that functions as a smartphone companion device.
- The strategy focuses on audio, video, and camera capabilities to reduce friction for content creators and social media users.
- Future iterations are expected to integrate Google’s Gemini AI for features like real-time translation and image editing.
- Success depends on appealing to Gen Z and fashion-conscious consumers through partnerships with brands like Warby Parker and Gentle Monster.
- Industry experts note that Google and Samsung must overcome Meta’s massive distribution advantage and established partnerships with eyewear giant EssilorLuxottica.

### Why it Matters
- This partnership represents a strategic effort to expand the Android ecosystem by creating "stickiness" through wearable hardware that keeps users engaged with Google services. By prioritizing fashion and practical utility over experimental tech, the companies hope to avoid the social stigma that led to the failure of the original Google Glass.
title: Can Google and Samsung redefine smart eyewear with Android XR, or will history repeat with a new generation of 'Glassholes'?

author: Kanika Gogia
summary:
Smart TV users experiencing persistent video buffering despite high-speed internet can resolve performance issues by testing hardware connections, clearing app cache, and switching from Wi-Fi to Ethernet.

### Key Points
- Smart TVs often feature weaker Wi-Fi antennas and less powerful processors compared to smartphones or laptops, leading to potential signal degradation.
- Comparing internet speeds across multiple devices helps determine if buffering is caused by the network, the streaming application, or the television hardware.
- Connecting a TV directly to a router via an Ethernet cable can significantly increase bandwidth and eliminate wireless interference.
- Accumulated temporary data in streaming applications can cause sluggish playback, which is often resolved by clearing the app cache in the settings menu.

### Why it Matters
Many consumers incorrectly blame their internet service providers for streaming issues, leading to unnecessary plan upgrades or hardware replacements. Identifying that the bottleneck is often internal to the TV allows users to optimize their existing equipment for a smoother viewing experience.
title: I ran 4 simple tests to diagnose my TV’s buffering problem, and my internet speed wasn’t to blame

author: MacRumors Staff
summary:
Apple has announced the schedule for its 2026 Worldwide Developers Conference and launched a new Apple Card promotion offering customers free AirPods Pro 3 with specific usage requirements.

### Key Points
- Apple’s WWDC 2026 is scheduled for June 8 through June 12, with an in-person keynote event held at Apple Park.
- New Apple Card users can earn free AirPods Pro 3 via a $25 monthly rebate for 10 months, provided they use the card 10 times each month.
- Development of a foldable iPhone has reportedly stalled due to hinge reliability issues failing to meet Apple's internal quality control standards.
- The iPhone 17 Pro was ranked as the fastest-charging smartphone overall in recent CNET lab testing.
- Apple re-released the Hikawa Grip & Stand accessory in three new colors, priced at $54.95, to support users with mobility and grip challenges.

### Why it Matters
These updates highlight Apple's ongoing efforts to incentivize financial service adoption while managing complex hardware engineering challenges for future product categories. The focus on accessibility and charging performance reflects the company's strategy to maintain market competitiveness through both software integration and hardware refinement.
title: Top Stories: Apple Card AirPods Pro Promo, WWDC 2026 Schedule, and More

author: Bradley C
summary:
A new macOS social engineering campaign called ClickFix highlights the urgent need for IT departments to shorten software update deferral windows to better protect against evolving security threats.

### Key Points
- The ClickFix campaign tricks users into pasting malicious scripts into Terminal via fake CAPTCHA or browser update alerts.
- Attackers use these scripts to steal macOS Keychain databases and browser session cookies, effectively bypassing multi-factor authentication.
- Apple introduced a native Terminal security warning in macOS Sequoia and macOS Tahoe 26.4 to disrupt these specific paste-based attacks.
- Industry experts recommend reducing the standard 90-day software update deferral window to 30 or 45 days to ensure critical security patches are applied.

### Why it Matters
Maintaining a 90-day update deferral window leaves corporate devices vulnerable to modern social engineering tactics that exploit known system weaknesses. Shortening this window is essential for IT teams to balance application compatibility testing with the immediate need to deploy critical OS-level security mitigations.
title: Apple @ Work: Why the ClickFix campaign means it is time to kill the 90 day update deferral

author: vkc
summary:
A Linux user converted an aging System76 laptop into a distraction-free "writerdeck" by installing a minimal Debian environment to focus exclusively on writing without modern internet interruptions.

### Key Points
- The setup utilizes a console-only Debian Trixie installation, removing all graphical desktop environments to eliminate software distractions.
- Essential tools include Neovim for text editing, Tmux for terminal multiplexing, and Vimwiki for organized note-taking.
- The system uses kmscon for scalable console rendering and Syncthing to securely sync writing files across devices.
- Custom configurations allow for hardware-specific features like battery monitoring via acpi and screen brightness control using the light package.
- Automatic login and boot-time scripts ensure the device launches directly into a writing session upon opening the laptop.

### Why it Matters
Creating a dedicated, single-purpose device helps users reclaim focus by removing the constant notifications and browser-based distractions inherent in modern computing. This approach demonstrates how repurposing older hardware with lightweight, open-source software can provide a highly intentional and productive environment for creative work.
title: It's time to talk about my writerdeck

author: Brady Snyder
summary:
Google has significantly expanded its AI Pro subscription bundle to include multiple premium services for $19.99 monthly, raising concerns about the long-term sustainability of the current pricing model.

### Key Points
- The Google AI Pro subscription now includes 5TB of cloud storage, Google Home Premium Standard, Google Health Premium, and YouTube Premium Lite.
- The bundle provides an estimated $50 in monthly value for a flat $19.99 fee, a significant increase in perks since October 2025.
- While the bundle price remains stable, Google has recently increased costs for individual services like YouTube Premium and Fitbit Premium.
- The aggressive bundling strategy appears designed to incentivize deeper consumer integration into the Google hardware and software ecosystem.

### Why it Matters
The rapid expansion of the AI Pro bundle suggests a strategic shift toward locking users into a unified ecosystem, potentially creating future price volatility. Consumers should remain cautious, as historical trends from competitors like Apple indicate that heavily discounted subscription bundles often face significant price hikes once market adoption is secured.
title: I have a bad feeling about the future of Google One prices

author: Lila Shroff
summary:
Computer science enrollment is declining as AI-driven automation disrupts entry-level programming roles, forcing universities to rethink how they prepare students for a rapidly evolving software industry job market.

### Key Points
- Undergraduate computer science enrollment dropped by over 8 percent last year, while graduate-level participation fell by 14 percent.
- Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark noted that approximately 90 percent of the company's new code is now generated by artificial intelligence.
- Despite higher unemployment rates for recent graduates, computer science majors continue to earn significantly higher wages than peers in most other fields.
- Universities like MIT have introduced dedicated AI majors, which have quickly become the second-most popular program on campus.
- Educators are divided on curriculum, with some emphasizing traditional manual coding fundamentals while others integrate AI tools directly into coursework.

### Why it Matters
The shift toward AI-generated code is fundamentally altering the career path for entry-level software developers and challenging the traditional value proposition of a computer science degree. As the industry prioritizes senior-level expertise over manual coding, educational institutions must adapt their curricula to ensure graduates remain relevant in an increasingly automated economy.
title: There’s Never Been a Better Time to Study Computer Science

author: Pierluigi Paganini
summary:
Ransomware groups are shifting away from system encryption in 2026, favoring quiet data theft and extortion to bypass security defenses and monetize stolen information through secondary criminal marketplaces.

### Key Points
- Ransom payment rates have dropped significantly, falling from 76% in 2019 to just 28% in 2026.
- Attackers are increasingly using "extortion-only" tactics, prioritizing the exfiltration of sensitive data over disruptive system encryption.
- Recent high-profile breaches include the theft of 3.65 TB of data from Instructure and significant exfiltration from Foxconn’s North American operations.
- Cybercriminals are now utilizing "Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver" (BYOVD) techniques to disable endpoint detection systems before initiating data theft.
- Stolen datasets are frequently sold to identity-fraud rings and intelligence services, turning leak sites into profitable marketplaces rather than simple coercion tools.

### Why it Matters
This strategic pivot renders traditional backup-and-restore recovery plans insufficient, as organizations can no longer rely on system restoration to mitigate the impact of a breach. Companies now face long-term liability and regulatory risks from public data exposure, which often persists long after the initial security incident is contained.
title: Why pure extortion is replacing traditional ransomware

author: Matt G. Southern
summary:
Google’s I/O 2026 announcements introduced advanced AI agents and updated search features, sparking industry debate over the future of traditional web traffic and the necessity of human-created content.

### Key Points
- Google integrated Gemini 3.5 Flash as the default AI model, with AI Mode now serving over one billion monthly users.
- New information agents will launch this summer for Pro and Ultra subscribers to monitor web updates and synthesize content for users.
- The updated search interface now accepts multi-modal inputs, including images, videos, files, and Chrome tabs, to better anticipate complex user intent.
- Research indicates that AI-driven search results can reduce organic clicks to external websites by up to 38% for specific query types.
- Google maintains that traditional search results and blue links remain accessible, contradicting claims that the standard search experience has been fully replaced.

### Why it Matters
The primary risk for publishers is not the disappearance of search links, but a significant decline in website traffic as AI agents synthesize information directly within Google. This shift forces content creators to prioritize unique, non-commodity analysis, as simple informational pages are increasingly satisfied without a user ever clicking through to the source.
title: Google I/O Didn’t End SEO. The Risk Is Somewhere Else via @sejournal, @MattGSouthern

author: staff@bgr.com (Chifundo Kasiya)
summary:
Securing your home router is essential to prevent cyberattacks, as 75% of IoT-targeted malware specifically exploits vulnerabilities in networking hardware like those manufactured by Netgear.

### Key Points
- Enable automatic firmware updates to ensure your router receives the latest security patches against known exploits.
- Change default SSIDs and admin passwords to prevent hackers from identifying your device model and using factory-set credentials.
- Implement MAC address filtering to restrict network access to only authorized devices via a whitelist or blacklist.
- Create a separate guest Wi-Fi network to isolate visitors and prevent potential malware from spreading to your primary devices.
- Disable Universal Plug and Play (UPnP) to close unnecessary ports that could allow unauthorized devices to communicate with your network.
- Use the WPA3 security protocol for stronger encryption and better protection against brute-force password attacks.

### Why it Matters
Routers serve as the primary gateway for your digital life, making them a high-value target for hackers seeking to steal data or recruit devices into botnets. Proactively hardening these settings significantly reduces the risk of unauthorized access and protects your personal information from sophisticated cyber threats.
title: 5 Easy Ways To Make Your Router More Secure

author: Phil Wahba
summary:
Hyper-fast fashion giant Shein has acquired the struggling apparel brand Everlane for $100 million, signaling a significant decline for companies built primarily on the promise of conscious consumerism.

### Key Points
- Shein purchased the debt-laden Everlane for $100 million, a sharp decrease from the brand's previous $600 million peak valuation.
- Allbirds, a sustainable footwear company, recently sold for $39 million, representing only 1% of its former peak market value.
- Beyond Meat has faced declining revenues and rebranded to focus on protein drinks after failing to sustain its initial growth as a meat alternative.
- Market analysts note that consumers increasingly prioritize price, convenience, and design over sustainability claims when making purchasing decisions.
- Competitors like Uniqlo and Quince have successfully captured market share by offering similar aesthetics without relying on virtue-based marketing strategies.

### Why it Matters
The decline of these brands highlights a growing disconnect between consumer values and actual spending habits in a challenging economic environment. It suggests that ethical branding is no longer a sufficient substitute for a strong, competitive value proposition in the retail sector.
title: The quiet death of conscious consumerism, from Everlane and Allbirds to Beyond Meat

author: beth.nicholls@futurenet.com (Beth Nicholls) , Beth Nicholls
summary:
The Cricut Joy 2 is a compact, portable vinyl cutting machine designed for beginners and crafters seeking an affordable, travel-friendly device for quick projects and creative customization.

### Key Points
- The machine weighs 2.7 lbs (1.2 kg) and features a maximum cut size of 4.5 inches by 48 inches when using smart materials.
- It is compatible with over 75 different materials and retails for $99, offering a budget-friendly alternative to larger flagship models.
- Setup is performed via Bluetooth through the Cricut Design Space software, which is available on mobile devices and laptops.
- The device is optimized for portability and small-scale crafting, though it lacks the engraving and debossing capabilities found in larger machines.

### Why it Matters
The Cricut Joy 2 lowers the barrier to entry for digital crafting by providing a space-saving, low-cost option that does not sacrifice precision. Its portability makes it a practical tool for hobbyists who need to work outside of a dedicated studio or workbench environment.
title: Tested: Cricut's new Joy 2 machine is teeny tiny (but mighty powerful), and I love it

author: Zack Whittaker
summary:
Health wearable manufacturer Oura faces scrutiny over its data security practices as the company declines to release transparency reports regarding government requests for sensitive user health information.

### Key Points
- Oura has sold over 5.5 million health-monitoring rings that track heart rate, sleep, location, and menstrual cycles.
- The company confirmed that user data is not end-to-end encrypted, allowing Oura staff to access information stored on its servers.
- Oura acknowledged receiving infrequent government requests for user data but refuses to disclose the frequency or nature of these demands.
- Despite previous indications that it was evaluating the release of a transparency report, Oura has remained unresponsive to recent inquiries on the matter.
- The company is currently valued at over $11 billion as it prepares for a potential public offering.

### Why it Matters
The lack of transparency regarding government data access creates significant privacy concerns for millions of users who entrust the company with intimate health metrics. As a market leader, Oura’s security architecture and refusal to disclose legal requests may impact consumer trust and set a precedent for data handling in the broader wearable technology industry.
title: Oura says it gets government demands for user data. Will it share how many?

author: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)
summary:
Anthropic’s Project Glasswing initiative has identified over 10,000 software vulnerabilities using the Claude Mythos Preview AI model, leading to 97 upstream patches and 88 security advisories issued globally.

### Key Points
- Project Glasswing provides 50 partners with early access to the Claude Mythos Preview model to autonomously detect critical software flaws.
- The initiative has confirmed 1,726 valid vulnerabilities, with 1,094 classified as high- or critical-severity across more than 1,000 open-source projects.
- A critical flaw in WolfSSL, identified as CVE-2026-5194 with a 9.1 CVSS score, was among the vulnerabilities discovered and addressed.
- The AI model successfully prevented a $1.5 million fraudulent wire transfer for a partner bank by detecting unauthorized account activity.
- Anthropic launched a Cyber Verification Program to allow security professionals to conduct red teaming and vulnerability research without standard AI guardrails.

### Why it Matters
The rapid discovery of vulnerabilities by AI models creates an urgent need for software vendors to accelerate their patch cycles and strengthen network defenses. As these advanced tools become more prevalent, organizations must prioritize faster remediation to stay ahead of potential exploitation by malicious actors.
title: Claude Mythos AI Finds 10,000 High-Severity Flaws in Widely Used Software

author: David Vellante, George Gilbert and David Floyer
summary:
The integration of artificial intelligence into enterprise software stacks is shifting business models from human-coordinated silos to automated, platform-like operations that prioritize shared truth and repeatable outcomes.

### Key Points
- Enterprises are moving beyond simple AI copilots to build a "System of Intelligence" that harmonizes data, business logic, and rules across fragmented departments.
- The new AI stack requires a "deterministic digital twin" to provide a foundation of truth, allowing probabilistic AI agents to act with confidence and governance.
- Companies like Palantir Technologies and Celonis are emerging as key players in building the necessary middle layer to integrate siloed enterprise applications.
- Adopting this model allows firms to scale revenue without proportional labor growth by automating the "human glue" of meetings, spreadsheets, and manual reconciliations.
- Dell Technologies is utilizing these principles to compress cross-functional decision-making cycles from weeks into minutes by leveraging a unified data mesh.

### Why it Matters
This transition represents a fundamental shift in how companies operate, moving away from labor-intensive coordination toward software-like marginal economics. By treating intelligence as a core production input, businesses can achieve greater agility and compounding competitive advantages that are difficult for rivals to replicate.
title: How AI stacks are rewriting the rules of business
Unitree GO-M8018-6 Motor Reverse Engineering
author: Maya Posch
summary:
Researcher Thomas Flayols is reverse-engineering the proprietary motor drivers of the Unitree Go2 quadruped robot to develop open-source firmware and bypass the manufacturer's restrictive software ecosystem.

### Key Points
- The Unitree Go2 utilizes compact, high-performance actuators featuring integrated reducers, magnetic encoders, and Cortex-M0-based CMS32M57xx microcontrollers.
- Thomas Flayols identified the obscured internal components by using X-ray imaging to bypass physical markings removed by the manufacturer.
- Researchers successfully extracted the encrypted firmware from the device's bootloader SRAM after recovering a local decryption key.
- The project aims to create custom, open-source firmware to allow broader use of these motors in independent robotics applications.

### Why it Matters
Developing open-source firmware for these actuators could significantly lower the barrier to entry for custom robotics projects by providing affordable, high-quality hardware. Furthermore, this initiative offers a potential security alternative for Go2 owners concerned about existing malware vulnerabilities and proprietary firmware restrictions.

title: Apple shares iPhone and Mac post-quantum cryptography code on GitHub
author: Marcus Mendes
summary:
Apple has released new corecrypto source code on GitHub, detailing its formal verification process for post-quantum cryptographic algorithms designed to protect user data against future quantum computing threats.

### Key Points
- Apple published source code for ML-KEM and ML-DSA algorithms, which align with NIST standards FIPS 203 and FIPS 204.
- The repository includes custom tools like a Cryptol-to-Isabelle translator used to verify that implementations match official cryptographic standards.
- Formal verification helped Apple identify and resolve critical errors, including a potential data corruption issue in an early ML-DSA implementation.
- The code supports both portable C and hand-optimized ARM64 assembly to ensure compatibility across the entire Apple silicon product lineup.
- This release follows the 2024 introduction of the PQ3 protocol for iMessage, marking a continued expansion of Apple's post-quantum security infrastructure.

### Why it Matters
This initiative represents a significant shift toward transparency in high-stakes cybersecurity, allowing independent researchers to audit the foundational code protecting Apple devices. By sharing these formal verification methods, Apple aims to set a higher standard for software assurance while proactively defending against the long-term risks posed by quantum computing.

title: 'It's clearly coming for us': Chris Hayes reveals 'front of mind' threat on new podcast
author: Matthew Chapman
summary:
MSNBC anchor Chris Hayes expressed significant concerns regarding the rapid integration of artificial intelligence, highlighting potential risks to the labor market, global warfare, and the integrity of political information.

### Key Points
- Chris Hayes discussed his apprehension toward AI integration during a recent interview with Public Notice journalist Thor Benson.
- The anchor identified the manipulation of political information through AI models like ChatGPT, Gemini, and Anthropic as a primary societal threat.
- Hayes cited Elon Musk’s Grok model as a specific example of how AI can be programmed to promote controversial conspiracy theories.
- Widespread grassroots opposition is currently challenging the expansion of AI infrastructure and data centers across the United States.
- Hayes emphasized the need for public awareness regarding how AI tools may influence future democratic processes and voter decision-making.

### Why it Matters
The rapid adoption of generative AI models is fundamentally changing how the public accesses information, raising concerns about the potential for political bias and misinformation. As these tools replace traditional search engines, the ability of tech companies to influence public discourse through model programming becomes a critical issue for democratic stability.

title: Weekly Wrap: Bitcoin Treads Water As Stocks Rally
author: CryptoProwl
summary:
U.S. equities reached near-record highs following Nvidia’s strong earnings, while the cryptocurrency sector saw increased regulatory scrutiny, new institutional investments, and expanded betting options on major private companies.

### Key Points
- The House Oversight and Government Reform Committee launched a probe into potential insider trading at prediction markets Kalshi and Polymarket.
- Ark Invest, led by Cathie Wood, purchased $12.5 million in Bullish stock as the crypto exchange's share price declined by 7%.
- Binance introduced a "Pre-IPO Perpetual Contract" allowing retail investors to speculate on the valuation of SpaceX ahead of its anticipated June public offering.
- Asset tokenization platform Securitize announced plans to go public through a merger with a special purpose acquisition company to trade on the Nasdaq.
- Bitcoin mining stocks, including Core Scientific and Hut 8, rose following Nvidia’s financial results due to their involvement in high-performance computing and AI data centers.

### Why it Matters
These developments highlight the growing intersection between traditional financial markets, artificial intelligence infrastructure, and the evolving regulatory landscape for digital assets. Increased oversight and institutional adoption suggest that crypto-related entities are becoming more deeply integrated into broader economic and political frameworks.

title: Forget iCloud. This Case Gives Your iPhone 2TB of Real Expandable SD Card Storage
author: Sarang Sheth
summary:
The iRe5 Gen 2 modular ecosystem provides iPhone and Android users with expandable microSD storage, pass-through charging, and professional-grade hub functionality to streamline mobile video production workflows.

### Key Points
- The system features two form factors: the aluminum X-Module for cinema rigs and a silicone Storage Case for daily carry.
- Both versions support up to 2TB of microSD expansion and USB 3.0 data transfer speeds reaching 360 MB/s.
- Advanced pass-through charging allows users to record 4K ProRes video while simultaneously powering the phone and connecting peripherals.
- The SyncPal companion app utilizes an NFC disc to trigger automated file backups and organization without manual menu navigation.
- The hardware is compatible with both USB-C and Lightning devices, supporting external SSDs and 3.5mm audio equipment.
- Pricing starts at $69.90 for the X-Module, with global shipping scheduled to begin in July 2026.

### Why it Matters
This modular solution addresses the growing friction creators face as flagship smartphones continue to remove physical storage slots and limit connectivity. By consolidating power, storage, and peripheral management into a single device, it reduces reliance on fragile dongles and cloud-based workflows during high-stakes professional shoots.

title: The sandworm malware strikes: How a hacker group stole 4,000 GitHub repositories and exposed the rot at the core of modern software security
author: Patrick Lewis
summary:
The hacker group TeamPCP has compromised 4,000 private GitHub repositories after infiltrating software supply chains through poisoned development tools, including VS Code extensions and trusted security scanners.

### Key Points
- TeamPCP is selling 4,000 stolen GitHub repositories for $50,000 after gaining access via a malicious VS Code extension.
- The group previously compromised the Trivy security scanner, allowing them to steal AWS keys, SSH credentials, and Kubernetes tokens from over 10,000 workflows.
- Attackers successfully breached Cisco Systems, cloning 300 repositories containing source code for unreleased AI products and sensitive data from government and banking clients.
- Malicious code propagated through the Bitwarden password manager and TanStack library by hijacking legitimate build pipelines and valid cryptographic signatures.
- The malware specifically targets AI assistant credentials, including those for Claude Code, marking a new trend in supply chain exploitation.

### Why it Matters
This breach exposes a systemic failure in the software industry's reliance on automated build tools and implicit trust in third-party development packages. Because the attackers use valid digital signatures to sign malicious code, traditional security verification tools are currently unable to distinguish between legitimate and compromised software updates.

title: 18 ways AI is rewriting the future of financial services: Key insights from the AWS Financial Services Symposium
author: Emile Louw
summary:
Financial institutions are rapidly shifting from experimental AI pilots to production-ready agentic systems to automate complex workflows, enhance operational efficiency, and improve real-time decision-making across global markets.

### Key Points
- Financial firms are prioritizing generative AI in 2025 budgets, with 41% of IT leaders focusing specifically on optimizing AI workflows already in production.
- AWS introduced Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, enabling AI agents to perform autonomous commercial transactions and payments in partnership with Coinbase and Stripe.
- Vanguard utilized Amazon Bedrock to automate document processing, reducing account inheritance cycle times from two weeks to a single day.
- Datadog and Dynatrace are providing unified observability platforms to help banks detect and remediate operational incidents automatically before they impact customers.
- Major institutions like Unum Group and various mortgage lenders are using agentic workflows to modernize millions of lines of legacy mainframe code in months rather than years.
- FICO and Numerix are deploying AI-powered decisioning tools to provide real-time risk assessment and personalized financial quotes, significantly reducing turnaround times for capital markets.

### Why it Matters
The transition toward agentic AI represents a fundamental shift in how financial services firms manage risk, compliance, and customer interactions. By moving beyond simple automation to integrated, autonomous platforms, these institutions are gaining the ability to respond to market volatility and operational demands at speeds previously impossible with legacy infrastructure.

title: Weekend A La Carte (May 23)
author: Tim Challies
summary:
The latest edition of the A La Carte newsletter explores the intersection of technology, theology, and culture, featuring expert commentary on artificial intelligence, labor, and religious traditions.

### Key Points
- Chris Griswold examines how artificial intelligence is fueling anxiety regarding the future of work and middle-class stability.
- Brad Littlejohn analyzes the concept of techno-feudalism and the growing influence of modern technology companies on global structures.
- A Wall Street Journal article explores the parallels between Karl Marx’s economic theories and traditional Christian eschatology.
- Tony Costa provides a theological critique of Eastern Orthodoxy, highlighting key doctrinal differences for Evangelical readers.
- Michael Graham discusses the epistemological implications of relying on foundational AI models for religious and primary knowledge.

### Why it Matters
These articles highlight the growing tension between rapid technological advancement and traditional belief systems in modern society. Understanding these shifts is essential for navigating the evolving landscape of digital ethics, labor markets, and cultural identity.

title: I bought a Lumix G1 for $50 — here’s how the world’s first mirrorless camera fares 18 years after launch
author: Timothy Coleman
summary:
To celebrate 25 years of Panasonic Lumix cameras, a tech journalist revisits the Lumix G1, the world’s first mirrorless camera, to evaluate its performance and relevance in 2026.

### Key Points
- The Panasonic Lumix G1, released 18 years ago, was the industry's first mirrorless camera to replace traditional DSLRs.
- Secondhand models are widely available, with complete kits often selling for as little as £80.
- The camera features a 12MP Micro Four Thirds sensor, a 3fps burst rate, and an ISO range limited to 100–3200.
- Despite its age, the G1 includes functional human subject-detection autofocus and a familiar, ergonomic DSLR-style design.
- The Micro Four Thirds system allows for cross-brand lens compatibility, enabling the use of modern lenses like the Olympus 45mm f/1.8.

### Why it Matters
The Lumix G1 serves as a historical benchmark for the mirrorless technology that currently dominates the professional and consumer photography markets. Its continued usability highlights the longevity of camera hardware and offers a budget-friendly entry point for enthusiasts interested in the evolution of digital imaging.

title: Save on your favorite tech over the 2026 Memorial Day weekend — grab deals on GPUs, gaming laptops, OLED monitors, and more
author: Stewart Bendle
summary:
The 2026 Memorial Day sales event is currently underway, offering significant discounts on gaming laptops, high-end PC components, monitors, and peripherals through Monday, May 25.

### Key Points
- Major retailers including Newegg, Amazon, Best Buy, Dell, Lenovo, HP, and Walmart are participating with discounts reaching up to 59%.
- Gaming hardware deals feature high-performance components like Nvidia RTX 50-series GPUs, Intel Core Ultra processors, and AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D CPUs.
- Monitor sales include QHD and OLED panels with refresh rates up to 240Hz from brands like Predator.
- Peripheral discounts cover popular gaming headsets and mechanical keyboards, such as the Arctis Nova Pro and Logitech G413 SE.
- Many deals require specific promotional codes, such as "PCGLOWUP25" for HP Omen configurations or "absgamer5" for ABS gaming PCs.

### Why it Matters
Memorial Day has evolved from a traditional holiday for home goods into a significant mid-year retail event for the technology and gaming sectors. These sales provide a strategic opportunity for consumers to upgrade hardware ahead of the larger Prime Day event later in the summer.

title: I fixed Android Auto lag with 3 tiny changes
author: Jorge Aguilar
summary:
Android Auto users experiencing lag, stuttering, or delayed touch responses can improve performance by adjusting battery optimization settings, reducing animation scales, and clearing accumulated app cache data.

### Key Points
- Set Android Auto battery usage to "Unrestricted" to prevent the operating system from killing background processes during use.
- Reduce window, transition, and animator duration scales to 0.5x within Developer Options to free up CPU resources.
- Clear the Android Auto app cache regularly to remove fragmented data that causes system bottlenecks and performance drops.
- Use high-quality, shielded USB 3.1 cables to ensure stable data transmission and reduce signal interference during wired connections.
- Disable background Wi-Fi scanning to minimize CPU interrupts and prevent thermal throttling during intensive projection tasks.

### Why it Matters
These adjustments help users resolve common connectivity issues without requiring new hardware or expensive vehicle upgrades. By optimizing how the smartphone manages resources, drivers can ensure a safer and more responsive navigation and entertainment experience on the road.

title: 340 newspapers block Wayback Machine. What that means for future of internet...
author: Mark Keierleber
summary:
More than 340 U.S. news publishers have blocked the Internet Archive’s Wayback Machine from indexing their content, citing concerns that AI companies may use the archives for model training.

### Key Points
- Over 340 national and local news outlets, including The New York Times, have restricted access to the Wayback Machine.
- The majority of these blocked sites are local newspapers owned by five major conglomerates, primarily USA Today.
- Publishers argue that blocking the tool protects their proprietary content and ensures access remains limited to paying subscribers.
- Internet Archive director Mark Graham maintains that the library is not a source for commercial AI scraping and warns that blocking it harms the public record.
- A petition signed by over 200 journalists advocates for the tool's preservation, citing its necessity for historical research and digital investigations.

### Why it Matters
The restriction of the Wayback Machine threatens the long-term preservation of digital history, as nearly 40% of web pages from a decade ago are already inaccessible. This conflict highlights the growing tension between media companies protecting their intellectual property and the public's need for an open, verifiable historical record.

title: Trump administration imposes 25% tariffs on advanced semiconductors to boost domestic manufacturing
author: Editorial Team
summary:
The Trump administration has implemented a 25% tariff on specific advanced semiconductors from Nvidia and AMD to incentivize domestic manufacturing and reduce reliance on foreign technology supply chains.

### Key Points
- The Presidential Proclamation, signed January 14, 2026, imposes a 25% tariff on advanced computing chips including Nvidia’s H200 and AMD’s MI325X processors.
- Tariffs were enacted following a national security investigation launched by the Commerce Department on April 1, 2025, under Section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act.
- A 90-day negotiation window concludes April 14, 2026, with potential for expanded tariffs on additional semiconductor products.
- Exemptions are available for chips used in US data centers, research, startups, consumer goods, and government applications.
- The administration plans to introduce an offset program to subsidize companies that invest directly in domestic semiconductor production facilities.

### Why it Matters
These tariffs represent a shift toward aggressive industrial policy by combining trade barriers with domestic subsidies to force a restructuring of the global semiconductor supply chain. Companies must now navigate complex compliance requirements or face increased costs, which could influence future hardware investment strategies across the technology sector.

title: Datadog CEO Touts AI, Cloud Migration as Revenue Growth Accelerates
author: MarketBeat
summary:
Datadog CEO Olivier Pomel reports that the company is experiencing sustained growth driven by cloud migration and the increasing complexity of artificial intelligence workloads across its customer base.

### Key Points
- Datadog reported 32% revenue growth, marking the fourth consecutive quarter of accelerated performance for the observability and security provider.
- The company currently holds a 13.6% share of the observability market, which leadership views as evidence of significant long-term growth potential.
- AI-native customer adoption is scaling rapidly, with 22 clients spending over $1 million and five spending more than $10 million annually.
- Datadog is expanding into AI training workloads, noting a shift from specialized, homegrown systems to broader infrastructure requirements among 50 to 100 companies.
- The Bits AI SRE tool has seen strong adoption, with 2,000 customers conducting over 100,000 investigations since the product's launch.

### Why it Matters
Datadog’s ability to capture value from both AI-native startups and traditional enterprises modernizing their infrastructure positions it as a critical utility in the evolving digital economy. By expanding its observability platform to include AI training and automated resolution, the company is securing a central role in managing the increasing complexity of modern software development.

title: Your next "Raspberry Pi project" doesn't actually need a Raspberry Pi
author: Ayush Pande
summary:
The evolving landscape of DIY computing now offers more cost-effective alternatives to Raspberry Pi single-board computers, including specialized microcontrollers for IoT projects and powerful x86 mini-PCs for demanding tasks.

### Key Points
- Microcontrollers like the ESP32 and Raspberry Pi Pico provide low-cost, energy-efficient solutions for sensor-based automation and IoT projects.
- ESP32 boards are preferred for smart home integrations, while the Raspberry Pi Pico excels in MicroPython and PIO-heavy experiments.
- Used x86 thin clients and mini-PCs offer superior processing power and software compatibility compared to mainline Raspberry Pi models.
- Modern x86 hardware, such as the N100, provides a performance-per-dollar advantage for users running complex applications or virtualization.
- The Raspberry Pi Zero remains a viable, budget-friendly option for projects that require more capability than a microcontroller but less power than a full PC.

### Why it Matters
The shift toward specialized hardware allows hobbyists to optimize their projects for either extreme power efficiency or high-performance computing. By moving away from a one-size-fits-all approach, users can significantly reduce costs while gaining access to broader software ecosystems and better hardware reliability.

title: As the AI Battle Rages, Yusuf Mehdi Becomes the Latest Microsoft Veteran to Walk
author: Robert Klara
summary:
Microsoft chief consumer marketing officer Yusuf Mehdi, a 35-year company veteran who oversaw the rollout of the Copilot AI assistant, has announced his planned departure for June 2027.

### Key Points
- Yusuf Mehdi has served as Microsoft’s chief consumer marketing officer since 2023, managing Windows, Microsoft 365, Surface, Edge, and Bing.
- Mehdi joined the company in 1992, playing key roles in the development of Internet Explorer, the Xbox One console, and the Windows product family.
- His departure follows a series of exits by long-term Microsoft executives, including Rajesh Jha, Julia Liuson, and former CMO Chris Capossela.
- Microsoft is currently navigating a transition toward AI, though adoption of the Copilot assistant remains low, with fewer than 4.5% of Microsoft 365 subscribers paying for the service.
- The company recently introduced its first voluntary retirement program for senior staff as it attempts to reduce corporate bureaucracy under CEO Satya Nadella.

### Why it Matters
The departure of a long-tenured executive like Mehdi signals a significant leadership transition as Microsoft shifts its focus toward aggressive AI integration. This change occurs while the company faces stiff competition in the generative AI market and internal pressure to streamline operations to remain agile against smaller, faster startups.

title: Xiaomi 17 Max gets disassembled on video
author: Vlad
summary:
Xiaomi has officially launched the 17 Max smartphone, a new flagship device featuring a Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 processor and an expansive 8,000 mAh battery capacity.

### Key Points
- The Xiaomi 17 Max features a 6.9-inch 1200x2608 LTPO AMOLED display with a 120Hz refresh rate and 3,500-nit peak brightness.
- Internal hardware includes the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 chipset, up to 16GB of RAM, and 512GB of internal storage.
- The camera system consists of a 200MP main sensor, a 50MP periscope telephoto lens with 3x optical zoom, and a 50MP ultrawide lens.
- The device supports 100W wired and 50W wireless charging, alongside 22.5W reverse wired and wireless charging capabilities.
- A detailed teardown video by the YouTube channel WekiHome provides a comprehensive look at the internal components of the new handset.

### Why it Matters
The introduction of the 17 Max expands Xiaomi's flagship lineup with a high-capacity battery that significantly exceeds standard industry benchmarks for premium smartphones. This release signals a strategic push to capture power-user segments by combining top-tier processing performance with extended battery longevity.

title: Trump Mobile Just Acknowledged The T1 Phone Database Leak Issue In The Worst Way Possible: Customers Still Not Notified
author: Rohail Saleem
summary:
Trump Mobile is currently evaluating whether to notify customers after a data leak exposed personal information belonging to individuals who pre-ordered the company's new T1 Phone.

### Key Points
- The data leak exposed customer names, email addresses, mailing addresses, cell phone numbers, and T1 Phone order identifiers.
- Trump Mobile attributed the security incident to an unnamed third-party platform provider rather than its own internal network or infrastructure.
- The T1 Phone, which retails for $499, is reportedly a rebranded HTC U24 Pro featuring a 6.78-inch screen and 512GB of storage.
- Despite the exposure of sensitive user data, the company has not yet issued a formal notification to the affected customers.

### Why it Matters
This incident highlights the significant security risks associated with third-party dependencies in the hardware supply chain and the importance of transparent crisis communication. Failure to promptly disclose data breaches can damage consumer trust and potentially lead to regulatory scrutiny regarding data protection standards.

title: Drupal Core SQL Injection Bug Actively Exploited, Added to CISA KEV
author: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)
summary:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency has added a critical SQL injection vulnerability in Drupal Core to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog following reports of active exploitation.

### Key Points
- The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-9082, carries a CVSS score of 6.5 and allows for potential privilege escalation and remote code execution.
- Security firm Imperva reported over 15,000 attack attempts targeting nearly 6,000 websites across 65 countries, primarily in the gaming and financial sectors.
- Drupal released patches for versions 11, 10, 9.5, and 8.9 to address the flaw within the database abstraction API.
- Federal Civilian Executive Branch agencies are required to apply the necessary security updates by the May 27, 2026, deadline.

### Why it Matters
This vulnerability poses a significant risk because it enables attackers to gain unauthorized access or control over websites running vulnerable PostgreSQL-backed configurations. Organizations using Drupal must prioritize patching immediately to prevent potential data breaches or full system compromise as exploitation attempts continue to rise.

title: Sketchy Imbalances In Data Training Are Distorting AI-Generated Mental Health Guidance
author:
Lance Eliot, Contributor, 
 Lance Eliot, Contributor
 https://www.forbes.com/sites/lanceeliot/
summary:
Generative AI models often provide flawed mental health advice because their training data is heavily skewed toward common experiences, causing them to overlook or misinterpret rarer, complex clinical conditions.

### Key Points
- Large language models (LLMs) like ChatGPT and Claude are trained on vast internet datasets that prioritize frequent, mainstream content over specialized medical knowledge.
- This data imbalance leads AI to favor "majority class" responses, often dismissing symptoms of serious conditions like hypomania as normal mood variations.
- Research published in *Patterns* highlights that such imbalances increase the risk of dangerous false negatives in critical fields like healthcare and finance.
- AI systems are often designed to be sycophantic and overly confident, which can mask their inability to accurately identify complex or edge-case mental health issues.
- Providing specific clinical context, such as DSM-5 criteria, can improve AI accuracy, but users cannot rely on these tools to consistently recognize severe symptoms.

### Why it Matters
The widespread use of AI as a 24/7 mental health advisor creates a significant public health risk because users often mistake the technology's confident tone for clinical authority. Without addressing these inherent data imbalances, AI platforms may inadvertently provide misleading guidance that delays professional intervention for individuals in crisis.

author: Mike Kershaw
title: This Week in Security: AI Generated Reports, More AI Generated Reports, GitHub Chaos, and More Linux Vulnerabilities
summary:
Google’s Project Zero discovered a zero-click exploit in Pixel 10 phones, while recent security disclosures highlight critical vulnerabilities across Linux kernels, NGINX servers, and major government infrastructure.

### Key Points
- Project Zero identified a memory access vulnerability in the Pixel 10 Tensor G5 chip, allowing remote-to-kernel escalation.
- GitHub suffered an internal repository breach after a developer’s credentials were stolen via a compromised VSCode extension.
- A CISA contractor accidentally exposed sensitive AWS GovCloud credentials and internal passwords in a public GitHub repository.
- The Linux kernel is removing complex zero-copy code from AF_ALG to mitigate recurring memory corruption exploits like CopyFail.
- A Chromium vulnerability, prematurely disclosed by Google, allows malicious JavaScript to maintain persistent background botnet activity in browsers.
- The NGINX "nginx-poolslip" vulnerability currently lacks an official patch, leaving millions of web servers potentially exposed to remote code execution.

### Why it Matters
These incidents underscore the growing risks associated with supply chain dependencies and the increasing difficulty of managing security at scale. As organizations and developers rely more on automated tools and third-party integrations, the potential for widespread system compromise through single points of failure continues to rise.

author: Dan DeFrancesco
title: The big strategy themes behind Microsoft's executive overhaul
summary:
Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella is restructuring the company’s senior leadership team and organizational hierarchy to accelerate the tech giant's strategic pivot toward artificial intelligence and operational efficiency.

### Key Points
- Microsoft is dismantling its existing senior leadership team to streamline decision-making processes under CEO Satya Nadella.
- Commercial chief marketing officer Yusuf Mehdi, a 35-year company veteran, is preparing to depart the organization.
- The reorganization emphasizes a "player-coach" model, requiring executives to engage directly with daily operations rather than focusing solely on high-level management.
- Nadella is actively replacing long-tenured executives with external hires to reduce institutional inertia and encourage new perspectives.
- The company is implementing a flatter structure to allow non-executive employees to contribute ideas directly during leadership meetings.

### Why it Matters
This leadership overhaul signals a significant cultural shift for one of the world's oldest and largest technology companies as it attempts to maintain its competitive edge in the AI era. By prioritizing agility and direct involvement, Microsoft aims to replicate the successful cloud-computing transition it achieved over the past decade.

author: Luke Larsen
title: Samsung Movingstyle M7 Review: A Screen on Wheels
summary:
Samsung has introduced the Movingstyle 32, a 32-inch 4K monitor bundled with a specialized rolling stand designed to provide flexible, portable display options for various home or office environments.

### Key Points
- The product combines an existing 4K Samsung monitor with a heavy-duty, five-caster rolling base for mobility.
- The system is designed to be easily moved between rooms, such as living areas, kitchens, or workspaces, as needed.
- Each purchase includes both the rollable stand and the standard monitor base, resulting in a large, heavy shipping package.
- The design prioritizes stability, utilizing a weighted base to balance the monitor while it is positioned on different surfaces.

### Why it Matters
This product addresses the growing consumer demand for flexible home technology that does not require a permanent, dedicated media room. By offering a portable display solution, Samsung provides a practical alternative for users who prioritize living space aesthetics without sacrificing high-resolution screen access.

author: Terrence O’Brien
title: Why would you disrespect your favorite artist with an AI remix?
summary:
Spotify has partnered with Universal Music Group to develop a generative AI tool that will allow premium subscribers to create and share remixes of songs from the label's catalog.

### Key Points
- Spotify and Universal Music Group (UMG) are collaborating on a new generative AI tool for music fans.
- The feature is intended to be a premium subscription add-on designed to deepen relationships between artists and listeners.
- Specific details regarding the tool's functionality, release date, and pricing remain unconfirmed by either company.
- UMG CEO Sir Lucian Grainge stated the initiative aims to foster fan engagement through creative experimentation with licensed music.

### Why it Matters
This partnership signals a significant shift in how major record labels approach generative AI, moving from litigation toward official licensing models. The move could redefine the relationship between streaming platforms and intellectual property, though it raises questions about the value of human artistry versus machine-generated content.

author: Tyler Graham
title: Xfinity Customers Still Have Time to Claim a Part of Comcast's $117.5M Data Breach Settlement
summary:
Comcast has agreed to a $117.5 million settlement to compensate approximately 36 million customers whose personal information was exposed during a significant data breach in October 2023.

### Key Points
- The settlement covers customers who received a breach notification from Comcast in December 2023 regarding the October 2023 incident.
- Eligible claimants can receive a flat $50 payment or up to $10,000 for documented losses and time spent addressing identity theft.
- Affected individuals must file a claim on the official settlement website by the September 14, 2026, deadline to receive compensation.
- Stolen data included usernames, passwords, names, contact information, dates of birth, and the last four digits of Social Security numbers.
- Comcast is also required to provide identity defense services to all members of the settlement class, regardless of whether they file a claim.

### Why it Matters
This settlement highlights the growing financial accountability major internet service providers face following large-scale cybersecurity failures. It provides a clear path for millions of consumers to seek restitution while underscoring the importance of monitoring personal data after corporate security breaches.

author: John Ruwitch
title: Check AI or just Google it? Google makes a big change to a little search box
summary:
Google is redesigning its iconic search box to integrate artificial intelligence, allowing users to perform multimodal searches and receive automated summaries alongside traditional web-based search results and links.

### Key Points
- The updated search interface supports longer, natural language queries and allows users to upload videos, images, and files for multimodal analysis.
- Google is introducing "agentic" functionality, enabling the search engine to perform ongoing tasks like tracking product prices or monitoring for local events.
- The integration aims to improve ad targeting by better understanding user intent through conversational search patterns.
- Critics warn that AI-generated summaries may reduce user agency, obscure information sources, and decrease traffic to third-party websites and news organizations.
- Concerns persist regarding the accuracy of AI outputs, citing previous instances where Google’s system provided incorrect or harmful advice.

### Why it Matters
This shift represents a fundamental change to the internet's primary gateway, potentially transforming how users discover information and interact with online businesses. By prioritizing AI-generated summaries over traditional link lists, Google risks disrupting the digital advertising and referral traffic models that sustain many web publishers.

author: Rob LeFebvre
title: These 5 Android permissions are basically a backdoor — never grant them to apps you don't trust
summary:
Android users should regularly audit app permissions to prevent malicious software from exploiting sensitive device access, such as accessibility services, location tracking, and SMS interception, to compromise personal data.

### Key Points
- Accessibility Services can be exploited by banking trojans like SpyNote to simulate user taps, intercept two-factor authentication codes, and bypass security prompts.
- The "Draw Over Other Apps" permission allows malicious software to overlay fake login screens on legitimate banking or password manager applications to steal credentials.
- Granting SMS permissions enables attackers to intercept one-time passwords and subscribe users to premium-rate services without their knowledge.
- Camera and microphone access can be abused by stalkerware or malicious updates to record ambient audio and video covertly, even when the app is not in use.
- Precise location access provides GPS-level tracking that can be used by ad networks or stalkerware to monitor a user's real-time movements and habits.

### Why it Matters
Managing these permissions is essential for maintaining device security and protecting sensitive financial and personal information from sophisticated mobile threats. By auditing access in the Android Privacy Dashboard, users can significantly reduce the risk of identity theft, unauthorized financial transactions, and covert surveillance.

author: Boing Boing's Shop
title: Avoid price discrimination with this 3-year VPN subscription for $68
summary:
Surfshark is offering a three-year subscription to its VPN service for $67.20, allowing users to bypass regional price discrimination and enhance online privacy while traveling or browsing at home.

### Key Points
- The three-year Starter Plan subscription is available for $67.20 using the discount code VPN20, a significant reduction from the standard $430 retail price.
- Surfshark provides access to over 3,200 servers across 100 countries, utilizing 10 Gbps infrastructure to maintain high connection speeds.
- Security features include AES-256 encryption, support for WireGuard and OpenVPN protocols, and a Bypasser tool for managing app-specific VPN traffic.
- The service supports unlimited simultaneous device connections across platforms including Windows, macOS, Android, iOS, and Linux.
- This promotional offer is valid through June 30 at 11:59 p.m. PT via the StackSocial marketplace.

### Why it Matters
Using a VPN allows travelers to circumvent location-based price gouging for flights and hotels by masking their IP address. This tool also provides essential digital security and privacy protections for users accessing the internet on multiple devices.

author: Sheryl Estrada
title: Inside Microsoft’s high-stakes push to win back its AI lead
summary:
Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella is pivoting the company’s strategy toward agentic AI tools like Copilot Tasks to regain market momentum following a 34% decline in share price since October.

### Key Points
- Microsoft shares dropped 34% between October 28, 2025, and March 27, 2026, amid a broader market repricing of subscription-software companies.
- CEO Satya Nadella has shifted his focus from commercial duties to direct involvement in AI research, product innovation, and data center infrastructure.
- The company is prioritizing Copilot Tasks, an autonomous agent designed to perform computer-based actions, to differentiate its AI stack from competitors.
- Wall Street analysts have labeled the current downturn in software valuations the "SaaSpocalypse," driven by the rise of AI coding agents that threaten traditional subscription models.

### Why it Matters
Microsoft’s struggle to maintain its early lead in the generative AI race highlights the intense pressure on legacy tech giants to prove the long-term profitability of their massive infrastructure investments. The company's pivot toward agentic software represents a critical attempt to defend its margins and per-seat economics against a rapidly evolving field of AI-native competitors.

author: sanujb6@gmail.com (Sanuj Bhatia) , Sanuj Bhatia
title: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt booed after AI remarks at the University of Arizona
summary:
Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt faced vocal opposition from University of Arizona students who booed his graduation speech regarding the inevitable and transformative role of artificial intelligence in society.

### Key Points
- Students at the University of Arizona graduation ceremony interrupted Eric Schmidt with boos during his remarks on artificial intelligence.
- Schmidt argued that AI represents a technological transformation more significant and rapid than any previous industrial shift.
